paragraphs.textJP: 最も基本的な形態では、UBIの保険料は走行距離に基づきます。ただし、PAYDはこれを超えるものです。PAYDでは、保険料は走行距離だけでなく、どのように、どこで、いつ運転するかにも依存します。PAYDでは保険料が動的に計算されます。保険料は走行量に応じて変動します。

paragraphs.text: There are three types of coverage afforded under UBI: (a) basic coverage based on the odometer reading of the vehicle (b) coverage derived from mileage aggregated from GPS data, or the duration of usage of the vehicle as recorded by a vehicle-independent module transmitting data via mobile devices or radio frequency (RF) technology (c) coverage based on other data collected from the vehicle, including speed, time of the day, historic road data, driving behaviour in addition to distance or duration travelled.
paragraphs.textJP:
UBIには3種類の補償形態があります：
(a) 車両の走行距離計の読み取りに基づく基本的な補償
(b) GPSデータから集計された走行距離、またはモバイルデバイスもしくは無線周波数（RF）技術を介してデータを送信する車両非依存型モジュールにより記録された車両の使用時間に基づく補償
(c) 距離または走行時間に加えて、速度、時間帯、過去の道路データ、運転行動など、車両から収集されたその他のデータに基づく補償

paragraphs.text: The last two types of coverage are via telematic UBI. This involves vehicle data being automatically transmitted to the system. It enables rapid feedback to the driver facilitating a dynamic insurance costing that varies with a change of risk. This incentivises drivers to drive more carefully. For example, if the driver stops driving for a period and works at home, this means a much lesser risk of motor accidents especially during peak traffic periods. With UBI, the savings in premium can be drastic not to mention immediate.
paragraphs.textJP: 後者の2種類はテレマティクスUBIを介して提供されます。これは車両データが自動的にシステムに送信されるもので、運転者への迅速なフィードバックを可能にし、リスクの変化に応じて変動する動的な保険料算出を促進します。これにより運転者のより慎重な運転が奨励されます。例えば、運転者がある期間運転を停止し在宅勤務する場合、特にピーク時の交通事故のリスクが大幅に低下します。UBIでは、保険料の節約は大きく、しかも即時的です。

paragraphs.text: As for PHYD (Pay How You Drive), it works like PAYD, but it also incorporates additional sensors like accelerometer to monitor driving behaviour.
paragraphs.textJP: PHYD（運転方法連動保険）はPAYDと同様に機能しますが、急ブレーキ、急加速、急ハンドルなどの運転行動を監視するための加速度センサーなどの追加センサーも組み込まれています。

paragraphs.text:
So, besides lower premiums, what benefits do UBI offer to motorists? The benefits include:
▪ Telematics technology track the status of the vehicle. Both the insured and the insurer are kept abreast of the safety and maintenance of the car
▪ Accident services can be offered. For example, emergency services can be dispatched. It is also possible to render on-the-spot claims assessment at the accident site.
▪ Driver safety awareness can be enhanced. Telematics are useful in tracking driving speeds. Road conditions and driver responses can be tracked as well.
paragraphs.textJP:
では、低い保険料以外にUBIは運転者にどのような利点を提供するのでしょうか？利点には以下が含まれます：
▪ テレマティクス技術が車両の状態を追跡します。被保険者と保険者の両方が車両の安全性と保守状況を常に把握できます
▪ 事故サービスを提供できます。例えば、緊急サービスを派遣できます。また、事故現場で即時の保険金請求評価を行うことも可能です
▪ 運転者の安全意識を向上させることができます。テレマティクスは運転速度の追跡に有用です。路面状況や運転者の反応も追跡できます

paragraphs.text: Electric cars are plug-in electric vehicles (EVs) which are environmentally friendly as they can reduce emissions. The impact of air pollution and emission of harmful gases is dependent on the type of vehicle as well as the source of electricity.
paragraphs.textJP: 電気自動車（EV）はプラグイン式電気自動車であり、排出量を削減できる環境に優しい車両です。大気汚染や有害ガスの排出への影響は、車両の種類と電力源に依存します。

paragraphs.text: Emission free EVs are usually charged with renewable energy like solar or wind. In general, electric cars cost more than conventional ones. However, the cost of maintaining an EV may be lesser as it helps owners save on petrol.
paragraphs.textJP: 排出ゼロのEVは通常、太陽光や風力などの再生可能エネルギーで充電されます。一般に、電気自動車は従来の自動車よりも購入価格が高いです。ただし、EVの維持費用はガソリン代の節約により低くなる場合があります。

paragraphs.text: With regards to EVs and insurance, the premiums are likely to be higher than conventional cars as it is more costly to repair electric cars given that they are damaged easily. However, the total amount that car owners pay for their EV insurance might be lower as there may be insurance discount for EVs.
paragraphs.textJP: EVと保険に関しては、電気自動車は損傷しやすく修理がより高価であるため、保険料は従来の自動車よりも高くなる可能性があります。ただし、EVに対する保険割引がある場合があるため、EV保険に対して自動車所有者が支払う総額はより低くなる可能性もあります。

paragraphs.text: One of the common issues that arise when it comes to claims on electric cars arises from the EV part that was damaged, which often results in an increase in the overall cost of claims. This can be substantiated by the fact that the parts perform more than one function as compared to parts in a conventional car. High costs can also be incurred due to the finding of right expertise to repair damages to the EV. As the technology for EVs are rather new, this also serves as one of the drivers for the high cost for this category of insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 電気自動車の保険金請求に関して生じる一般的な問題の一つは、損傷したEV部品に関するものであり、多くの場合、保険金請求の全体的なコストの増加につながります。これは、EVの部品が従来の自動車の部品と比較して複数の機能を果たすという事実に裏付けられます。EVへの損傷を修理するための適切な専門技術を見つけることによっても高額なコストが発生する可能性があります。EVの技術はかなり新しいため、この保険カテゴリの高コストの要因の一つにもなっています。

paragraphs.text: Another major factor that determines the rate for car insurance for EVs is the type of batteries used in the electrical car. Repair costs may be higher due to the damaged or leaking batteries which typically require extra safety precautions. However, such repair costs are decreasing according to studies.
paragraphs.textJP: 電気自動車の保険料率を決定するもう一つの主要な要因は、電気自動車に使用されるバッテリーの種類です。損傷したまたは漏洩したバッテリーは通常、追加の安全対策を必要とするため、修理費用がより高くなる場合があります。ただし、研究によるとそのような修理費用は減少傾向にあります。

subsections.label: H2
subsections.title: Electric Car Sharing
subsections.titleJP: 電気自動車シェアリング

paragraphs.text: BlueSG is a Singapore based company that provides local electric car sharing services. The company has about a thousand shared electric Bluecar vehicles that are located around the island, such as HDBs and commercial estates.
paragraphs.textJP: BlueSGはシンガポールに拠点を置き、地域の電気自動車シェアリングサービスを提供する企業です。同社はHDB団地や商業施設周辺など島内各所に約1,000台の共有電気自動車Bluecarを保有しています。

paragraphs.text: Users of BlueSG are insured under the Insurance Cover provided by BlueSG. The Insurance Cover insures the user against liability against a third party caused by or arising out of a BlueSG car on the road in accordance with the terms of the Contract, in respect of death, personal injury and/or damage to property suffered by such third party, pursuant to the Motor Vehicles (Third- Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960.
paragraphs.textJP: BlueSGの利用者は、BlueSGが提供する保険（Insurance Cover）に基づいて付保されています。この保険は、自動車（第三者リスクおよび補償）法1960年に基づき、BlueSGの車両が道路上で契約の条件に従って引き起こした、または生じた第三者に対する賠償責任（第三者が被った死亡、人身傷害および／または財物損害に関するもの）を補償します。

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Motor Claims Framework (MCF)
subsections.titleJP: 自動車保険金請求制度（MCF）

paragraphs.text: The GIA initiated the industry-wide Motor Claims Framework (MCF) in Singapore on 1 June 2008, to provide clear and common procedures for the reporting of motor accidents. All insurers in Singapore fully support the MCF. The MCF provides motorists with an enhanced claims experience and helps contain claims costs for insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポール損害保険協会（GIA）は2008年6月1日に業界全体の自動車保険金請求制度（MCF）を導入しました。これは、自動車事故の届出に関する明確で共通の手続きを提供するためです。シンガポールのすべての保険者がMCFを全面的に支持しています。MCFは運転者の保険金請求体験を向上させ、保険者の保険金請求コストの抑制に役立ちます。

paragraphs.text: In the event of an accident, the insured should avoid all unauthorised tow-truck operators and repair workshops.
paragraphs.textJP: 事故が発生した場合、被保険者は無認可のレッカー車業者および修理工場を利用しないようにしなければなりません。

事故の当事者間で以下の情報を交換してください：
▪ 氏名
▪ NRIC／FIN番号
▪ 電話番号
▪ 住所
▪ 保険者名

paragraphs.text: Take note of the vehicle numbers.
paragraphs.textJP: 相手方車両の登録番号を記録してください。

paragraphs.text:
Take pictures at the accident scene and submit all pictures to the motorist’s authorised accident reporting centre when reporting the accident. (a) What to photograph?
▪ Scene: Take pictures of the accident scene capturing the accident vehicles and surrounding areas.
▪ Own Car: Take pictures of the damages to the motorist’s own car and capture the licence plate.
▪ Other Damaged Car(s)/Vehicle(s): Take pictures of damages to all other cars/vehicles involved in the accident capturing licence plates. For multiple cars/vehicles collision, take pictures of those with direct contact, e.g. immediate front and immediate rear cars/vehicles.
paragraphs.textJP:
事故現場で写真を撮影し、事故を届け出る際にすべての写真を運転者の認定事故報告センターに提出してください。
(a) 何を撮影すべきか？
▪ 現場：事故車両と周辺地域を捉えた事故現場の写真を撮影
▪ 自車：自車の損傷と登録番号（ナンバープレート）の写真を撮影
▪ 相手方の損傷車両：事故に関与したすべての車両の損傷と登録番号の写真を撮影。複数車両の衝突の場合、直接接触した車両（直前の車両と直後の車両等）の写真を撮影

paragraphs.text: Call the insurer’s hotline for a tow truck or for further advice on the accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者のホットラインに電話し、レッカー車の手配または事故に関するアドバイスを求めてください。

paragraphs.text: Report and bring the accident car (whether damaged or not) to the approved reporting centre/authorised workshop within 24 hours or by the next working day.
paragraphs.textJP: 事故車両（損傷の有無を問わず）を24時間以内または翌営業日までに認定報告センター／認定修理工場に届け出て持ち込んでください。

paragraphs.text: Under the MCF, insurers assist policyholders in handling repairs of the damaged vehicle but will not file a third-party claim on the policyholder’s behalf. However, their approved reporting centres can assist them to file a thirdparty claim.
paragraphs.textJP: MCFに基づき、保険者は保険契約者の損傷車両の修理を支援しますが、保険契約者に代わって第三者への請求を提出することはありません。ただし、保険者の認定報告センターが第三者への請求の提出を支援することは可能です。

paragraphs.text: Insurers will provide a comprehensive service (via their approved reporting centres or workshops) to encourage the insured to report his accident with his accident car/vehicle as well as have the repairs carried out at the approved workshops.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、被保険者が事故車両とともに事故を届け出て認定修理工場で修理を行うことを奨励するために、認定報告センターまたは修理工場を通じて包括的なサービスを提供します。

paragraphs.text: By reporting the accident within the stipulated time, the motorist enjoys hasslefree and quality-certified repair services as provided under the MCF and avoids potentially higher costs from exaggerated claims.
paragraphs.textJP: 所定の期間内に事故を届け出ることにより、運転者はMCFに基づき提供される煩わしさのない品質保証付きの修理サービスを享受でき、誇大な請求による潜在的なコスト増を回避できます。

subsections.label: A3
subsections.title: Consequences For Failure To Report An Accident To The Insurer
subsections.titleJP: 保険者への事故届出を怠った場合の結果

paragraphs.text: If the motorist fails to report an accident to his insurer within the stipulated period of 24 hours or by the next working day after the accident, he may face any of the following consequences: (a) His insurer may repudiate liability because the insured has breached the policy condition to report an accident. Therefore, the policy will not provide protection and the insured will have to deal with his own repair costs and any third-party claims made against him. (b) His No Claims Discount will be reduced upon the renewal of his policy. (c) His insurer may cancel or decline renewal of his policy if he blatantly ignores the insurer’s requests to file an accident report.
paragraphs.textJP:
運転者が事故発生後24時間以内または翌営業日までに保険者に事故を届け出なかった場合、以下のいずれかの結果に直面する可能性があります：
(a) 被保険者が事故を届け出るという保険証券の条件に違反したため、保険者は賠償責任を否認する場合があります。したがって、保険証券は保護を提供せず、被保険者は自身の修理費用および相手方からの請求に対して自ら対処しなければなりません。
(b) 保険証券の更新時にNCDが引き下げられます。
(c) 被保険者が事故報告書の提出に関する保険者の要請を著しく無視した場合、保険者は保険証券を解約するか更新を拒否する場合があります。

subsections.label: A4
subsections.title: Does The Motorist Have To Inform His Insurer Even Though There Is No
subsections.titleJP: 車両に損傷がなくても保険者に届け出る必要があるか？

paragraphs.text: Damage To His Car?
paragraphs.textJP: 車両に損傷がなくても保険者に届け出る必要があるか？

paragraphs.text: Yes, he must always report all accidents to his insurer no matter how minor the accident may be, even if there is no visual damage or even if he has made a private settlement with the other driver.
paragraphs.textJP: はい、事故がどんなに軽微であっても、目に見える損傷がなくても、あるいは相手方の運転者と私的に示談を成立させた場合であっても、すべての事故を常に保険者に届け出なければなりません。

paragraphs.text: This is because he cannot be certain that the other party will not file a claim against him later. He may find himself in a position of having his claim prejudiced or declined later by insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: これは、相手方が後日請求を提出しないという確証がないためです。届出を怠った場合、保険者により後に請求が不利に扱われたり拒否されたりする立場に置かれる可能性があります。

subsections.label: A5
subsections.title: What Happens If The Motorist Chooses Not To Report The Accident To His
subsections.titleJP: 事故を保険者に届け出ないとどうなるか？

paragraphs.text: Insurer?
paragraphs.textJP: 事故を保険者に届け出ないとどうなるか？

paragraphs.text: Failure to do so will affect his NCD upon renewal of his motor insurance and will prejudice his claim.
paragraphs.textJP: 届出を怠った場合、自動車保険の更新時にNCDが影響を受け、請求も不利になります。

subsections.label: A6
subsections.title: If The Motorist Brings His Accident Vehicle To The Authorised Reporting
subsections.titleJP: 事故車両を認定報告センター・修理工場に持ち込んだ場合の費用は？

paragraphs.text: Centre Or Workshop, How Much Does He Has To Pay For The Inspection, Survey And Accident E-Filing Process?
paragraphs.textJP: 事故車両を認定報告センターまたは修理工場に持ち込んだ場合、検査、調査および事故電子届出手続きにいくらかかりますか？

paragraphs.text: There are no fees or charges involved in the reporting process.
paragraphs.textJP: 届出手続きに関する料金や費用は一切かかりません。

paragraphs.text: Motor insurance fraud involves conspiring to make false or exaggerated claims involving property damage or personal injuries. Some common examples include staged accidents, where fraudsters deliberately “arrange” for accidents to occur, the use of phantom passengers where persons who were not at the scene of the accident claimed to have suffered grievous injury, and making false personal injury claims where personal injuries are grossly exaggerated.
paragraphs.textJP: 自動車保険詐欺とは、共謀して自動車事故に関連する財物損害または人身傷害について、虚偽のまたは誇大な請求を行うことです。一般的な例として、詐欺師が意図的に事故を「仕組む」ステージドアクシデント、事故現場にいなかった者が重傷を負ったと主張するファントムパッセンジャー（幽霊乗客）の使用、および人身傷害が著しく誇大に主張される虚偽の人身傷害請求などがあります。

paragraphs.text: In March 2019, the General Insurance Association of Singapore (GIA) introduced the GIA Insurance Fraud Tip-off (GIFT) scheme which rewards individuals up to S$10,000 - based on the equivalent value of the fraudulent claim - for reporting insurance fraud cases that lead to successful prosecution and conviction of offenders. If there is more than one informant for a case, the panel of investigators will decide on the reward allocation to each informant.
paragraphs.textJP: 2019年3月、シンガポール損害保険協会（GIA）は保険詐欺通報制度（GIFT）を導入しました。この制度は、犯罪者の訴追および有罪判決につながる保険詐欺事案を通報した個人に対し、不正請求の同等額に基づき最大S$10,000の報奨金を支払うものです。一つの事案に対して複数の通報者がいる場合、調査委員会が各通報者への報奨金の配分を決定します。

paragraphs.text: GIFT is part of GIA’s efforts to increase participation of all stakeholders - including general insurers, regulators, and members of the public - to collectively combat insurance fraud and mitigate claims costs inflation.
paragraphs.textJP: GIFTは、損害保険会社、規制当局、一般市民を含むすべてのステークホルダーの保険詐欺対策への参加を促進し、保険金請求コストのインフレを軽減するGIAの取り組みの一環です。

paragraphs.text: GIA introduced this scheme to encourage members of the public to play a more pro-active role in tackling this problem together so that insurance in Singapore remains accessible. Members of the public who have been approached to get involved in insurance fraud or are currently holding relevant evidence of others committing fraud should participate in the scheme.
paragraphs.textJP: GIAは、シンガポールの保険が引き続き利用しやすいものであるよう、一般市民がこの問題に対してより積極的な役割を果たすことを奨励するためにこの制度を導入しました。保険詐欺に関与するよう働きかけられた、または他者の詐欺行為の関連証拠を現在保有している一般市民は、本制度に参加すべきです。

paragraphs.text: To qualify for the reward scheme, individuals are required to provide documentary evidence such as detailed descriptions of how the fraud was conducted, electronic document trails, actual copies of forged or tampered documents, or any another physical evidence that can prove the suspected fraud.
paragraphs.textJP: 報奨制度の対象となるためには、詐欺がどのように行われたかの詳細な説明、電子文書の証跡、偽造または改ざんされた文書の実際のコピー、または疑われる詐欺を証明できるその他の物的証拠などの証拠書類の提出が求められます。

paragraphs.text: The GIFT scheme builds on the success of the GIA Fraud Management System (FMS) which employs data analytics and artificial intelligence to detect fraud cases for motor and travel insurance. The system which was implemented in 2017 has since played a key role in helping the industry mitigate underwriting losses for the motor insurance segment.
paragraphs.textJP: GIFT制度は、自動車保険および旅行保険の詐欺事案を検出するためにデータ分析と人工知能を活用するGIA詐欺管理システム（FMS）の成功に基づいています。2017年に導入されたこのシステムは、自動車保険セグメントの引受損失の軽減に業界が取り組む上で重要な役割を果たしています。

sections.title: Motor Insurance – Related Issues And Organisations
sections.titleJP: 自動車保険関連の問題と組織

paragraphs.text: Set up by insurers on 22 February 1975, the Motor Insurers’ Bureau (MIB) is an independent body supported by all motor insurers in Singapore. Its main purpose is to compensate people injured in road accidents caused by negligent untraced or uninsured motorists. The MIB only provides cover for bodily injury claims in accordance with the Untraced Drivers' Agreement and the Uninsured Drivers' Agreement made by the Government, the MIB, and the general insurance companies.
paragraphs.textJP: 1975年2月22日に保険者により設立されたシンガポール自動車保険者機構（MIB）は、シンガポールのすべての自動車保険者が支援する独立機関です。その主な目的は、過失のある身元不明または無保険の運転者が引き起こした交通事故で負傷した人々に補償を提供することです。MIBは、政府、MIBおよび損害保険会社が締結した身元不明運転者協定および無保険運転者協定に基づき、身体傷害の請求についてのみ補償を提供します。

paragraphs.text: The Untraced Drivers' Agreement requires the MIB to consider compensation for victims of hit-and-run accidents where the vehicles are untraced. The Uninsured Drivers' Agreement requires the MIB to meet unsatisfied Court Judgements against identified motorists who are uninsured.
paragraphs.textJP: 身元不明運転者協定に基づき、MIBは車両の身元が特定できない「ひき逃げ」事故の被害者に対する補償を検討することが求められています。無保険運転者協定に基づき、MIBは無保険であると特定された運転者に対する未履行の裁判所判決を履行することが求められています。

paragraphs.text: The MIB Council, which comprises representatives from the leading motor insurers and the Government, assesses all claims and determines their validity.
paragraphs.textJP: 主要な自動車保険者および政府の代表者で構成されるMIB理事会が、すべての請求を審査し、その妥当性を判断します。

paragraphs.text: The MIB deals with all claims in accordance with service standards. However, if the claimant is dissatisfied, he can write to the MIB through his solicitor. The MIB will then forward his appeal to the Public Trustee, whose decision is final.
paragraphs.textJP: MIBはサービス基準に従ってすべての請求を処理します。ただし、請求者が不満を持つ場合、弁護士を通じてMIBに書面で異議を申し立てることができます。MIBはその後、公共受託者（Public Trustee）に上訴を転送し、その決定は最終的なものとなります。

paragraphs.text: The MIB obtains its funding from a levy imposed on its members, and membership is compulsory for all motor insurers in Singapore. This levy is based on each member’s motor premium income in proportion to the aggregate motor premium income in the general insurance market.
paragraphs.textJP: MIBはメンバーに課す分担金により資金を得ており、シンガポールのすべての自動車保険者に加入が義務づけられています。この分担金は、損害保険市場における自動車保険料収入の合計に対する各メンバーの自動車保険料収入の割合に基づきます。

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: Non-Injury Motor Accident Reporting Scheme
subsections.titleJP: 非傷害自動車事故届出制度

paragraphs.text: Contrary to common belief, there is no necessity for motorists to report every motor accident to the police, especially if the accident involves a “non-injury” situation.
paragraphs.textJP: 一般的な誤解に反して、運転者がすべての自動車事故を警察に届け出る必要はありません。特に「非傷害」事故の場合はそうです。

自動車事故が発生した場合、現行の「非傷害自動車事故届出制度」（1999年5月1日にGIAがシンガポール交通警察と協力して導入）の下では、以下の場合を除き警察届出は不要です：
▪ 傷害を伴う事故
▪ 政府車両の関与または政府財産の損害
▪ 外国車両の関与
▪ 歩行者または自転車利用者の関与
▪ 「ひき逃げ」事故

paragraphs.text:
An “injury” accident is defined as one in which:
▪ at least one party involved in the motor accident is conveyed to the hospital from the accident scene by an ambulance; or
▪ conveyance to the hospital is done through other transport, and the party is subsequently hospitalised, or given outpatient medical leave of three days or more; or
▪ no one is conveyed to the hospital from the scene, but a latent injury has subsequently developed which entailed hospitalisation, or outpatient medical leave of three days or more.
paragraphs.textJP:
「傷害」事故とは以下のように定義されます：
▪ 事故に関与した当事者の少なくとも1名が事故現場から救急車で病院に搬送された場合
▪ 他の交通手段で病院に搬送され、その後入院したか、3日以上の外来診療休暇を付与された場合
▪ 現場からは誰も病院に搬送されなかったが、その後潜在的な傷害が発現し、入院または3日以上の外来診療休暇を要した場合

paragraphs.text: The motorists involved in non-injury accidents need to exchange only their details, complete the “GIA Non-Injury Motor Accident Report Form”, and submit it to their insurers. The insurers will process this Form and submit it to the GIA Record Management Centre system via e-filing. This can also be done through one of the approved reporting centres or authorised workshops. 3 Foreign vehicles are defined as all vehicles registered in another country, all vehicles owned by visiting forces present in Singapore, all vehicles with USN or USG prefixes etc.
paragraphs.textJP:
非傷害事故に関与した運転者は、詳細を交換し、「GIA非傷害自動車事故報告書」に記入した上で、保険者に提出するだけで済みます。保険者はこの報告書を処理し、電子届出によりGIA記録管理センターシステムに提出します。これは認定報告センターまたは認定修理工場を通じても行うことができます。
※ 外国車両とは、他国で登録されたすべての車両、シンガポールに駐留する来訪軍のすべての車両、USNまたはUSGの接頭辞を持つすべての車両等と定義されます。

subsections.label: C
subsections.title: Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre Ltd (FIDReC)
subsections.titleJP: 金融業界紛争解決センター（FIDReC）

paragraphs.text: In this section, we will examine the role of FIDReC, including the set-up of FIDReC and its dispute resolution process.
paragraphs.textJP: このセクションでは、FIDReCの役割（FIDReCの設立および紛争解決プロセスを含む）について説明します。

paragraphs.text: FIDreC was launched in August 2005, as part of an initiative by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to create a one-stop centre for the resolution of all retail disputes with financial institutions.
paragraphs.textJP: FIDReCは2005年8月に、シンガポール金融管理庁（MAS）のイニシアティブの一環として設立されました。金融機関との消費者のすべてのリテール紛争を解決するためのワンストップセンターです。

paragraphs.text: FIDReC’s mission is to provide an affordable alternative dispute resolution scheme that is independent and impartial, so as to encourage and assist in the resolution of disputes between consumers and financial institutions in an amicable and fair manner. Disputes lodged with FIDReC are usually resolved within 6 months, without the involvement of external lawyers.
paragraphs.textJP: FIDReCの使命は、消費者と金融機関の間の紛争を友好的かつ公正な方法で解決することを奨励・支援するために、手頃で独立かつ公平な代替的紛争解決制度を提供することです。FIDReCに提出された紛争は通常、外部の弁護士の関与なく6か月以内に解決されます。

paragraphs.text: FIDReC's jurisdiction is set out in its Terms of Reference which can be found at its website. There is no claim limit for mediation at FIDReC, but the jurisdiction for adjudications at FIDReC is up to $150,000 per claim (for claims filed on or after 1st July 2024).
paragraphs.textJP: FIDReCの管轄は、そのウェブサイトに掲載されている付託条件に定められています。FIDReCでの調停に請求金額の上限はありませんが、FIDReCでの裁定の管轄は1請求あたり$150,000までです（2024年7月1日以降に申し立てられた請求について）。

paragraphs.text: Generally, FIDReC only handles disputes between financial institutions who are its subscribers and consumers. The financial institution must also have had the opportunity to resolve the dispute with the consumer first.
paragraphs.textJP: 一般に、FIDReCは加盟金融機関と消費者間の紛争のみを扱います。金融機関は、まず消費者との紛争を解決する機会を持っていなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: The following qualify as consumers: (a) Individuals or sole proprietors who have a customer relationship with a financial institution; (b) Persons who have a beneficial interest in the activity; (c) A trustee or personal representative; (d) An insured; or (e) Third parties who have third-party coverage under an insurance contract.
paragraphs.textJP:
以下が消費者に該当します：
(a) 金融機関と顧客関係を有する個人または個人事業主
(b) 当該活動に受益権を有する者
(c) 受託者または法定代理人
(d) 被保険者
(e) 保険契約に基づく第三者補償の対象である第三者

paragraphs.text: The following complaints cannot be brought before FIDReC: (a) Commercial Decisions; (b) pricing policies and other policies such as interest rates and fees; (c) cases relating solely to investment performance of a financial product; (d) cases under investigation by any law enforcement agency, including cases where allegations of fraud or criminal activity have been made, and where the matter has been referred to the police for investigation; (e) disputes between an FI and its officers and employees relating to agency or employment issues; (f) Complaints that are more than six months old after the FI’s final reply; (g) Complaints arising under a Former Scheme and which have been considered by or resolved under that Scheme; (h) Complaints that have been dealt with by or resolved by FIDReC unless there is new material information that was not reasonably available at the time the previous complaint had been filed; (i) Complaints that have been settled privately or otherwise between the Eligible Complainant and the FI; and (j) cases which have been subjected to a court hearing and for which a court judgment and/or order has been passed.
paragraphs.textJP:
以下の苦情はFIDReCに提起できません：
(a) 商業的判断
(b) 金利や手数料などの価格設定方針およびその他の方針
(c) 金融商品の投資パフォーマンスのみに関する事案
(d) いかなる法執行機関による調査中の事案（詐欺や犯罪行為の申立てがなされ、警察に捜査が付託された事案を含む）
(e) 代理または雇用問題に関する金融機関とその役職員・従業員間の紛争
(f) 金融機関の最終回答後6か月以上経過した苦情
(g) 旧制度の下で発生し、その制度で検討済みまたは解決済みの苦情
(h) FIDReCが処理済みまたは解決済みの苦情（前回の苦情申立て時に合理的に入手できなかった新たな重要情報がある場合を除く）
(i) 適格な苦情申立人と金融機関の間で私的にまたはその他の方法で和解された苦情
(j) 裁判所の審理を受け、裁判所の判決および／または命令が下された事案

paragraphs.text: Additionally, please also take note that FIDReC can dismiss a dispute without mediation in various situations, including where: (a) it is in the opinion of the Case Manager frivolous or vexatious; or (b) there are other compelling reasons why it is inappropriate for FIDReC to deal with the dispute.
paragraphs.textJP:
また、FIDReCは以下の場合を含むさまざまな状況において、調停なしに紛争を棄却できることにご留意ください：
(a) ケースマネージャーの見解により、不当または嫌がらせ目的である場合
(b) FIDReCが紛争を扱うことが不適切であるその他のやむを得ない理由がある場合

paragraphs.text: The dispute resolution process of FIDReC comprises the following stages: (a) Complaint Filing (1st Stage) Consumer lodges complaint at FIDReC and Case Manager verifies if the complaint is within FIDReC’s jurisdiction. (b) Mediation (2nd Stage) When a complaint is first received, the Case Manager will arrange mediation to facilitate discussions between disputants with the objective of reaching a settlement. For fees and costs details, please see table below. (c) Adjudication (3rd Stage) Where the dispute is not settled by mediation, the consumer can choose to allow an Adjudicator to decide the case on the facts and merits. For fees and costs details, please see table below.
paragraphs.textJP:
FIDReCの紛争解決プロセスは以下の段階で構成されています：
(a) 苦情受付（第1段階）
消費者がFIDReCに苦情を申し立て、ケースマネージャーが苦情がFIDReCの管轄内であるかを確認します。
(b) 調停（第2段階）
苦情が受理されると、ケースマネージャーは和解の達成を目的として紛争当事者間の協議を促進するための調停を手配します。手数料および費用の詳細については以下の表を参照してください。
(c) 裁定（第3段階）
調停で紛争が解決しなかった場合、消費者は裁定官に事実と理由に基づいて事案を判断させることを選択できます。手数料および費用の詳細については以下の表を参照してください。

paragraphs.text: The decision of the Adjudicator or Panel is final and binding on the financial institution, but not on the consumer. If the consumer is not satisfied with the decision, he has the option to continue to pursue the complaint through other channels or means such as litigation.
paragraphs.textJP: 裁定官またはパネルの判断は金融機関に対して最終的かつ拘束力を有しますが、消費者に対しては拘束力を有しません。消費者がその判断に不満の場合、訴訟などの他の手段を通じて苦情を引き続き追求することができます。

paragraphs.text: The FIDReC Non-Injury Motor Accident Scheme (FIDReC-NIMA Scheme) helps consumers resolve non-injury motor accident disputes with insurance companies in which the amount claimed is below S$3,000. The FIDReC-NIMA Scheme covers claims by complainants against a third-party insurance company.
paragraphs.textJP: FIDReC非傷害自動車事故制度（FIDReC-NIMA制度）は、消費者が保険会社との間で請求額がS$3,000未満の非傷害自動車事故紛争を解決することを支援します。FIDReC-NIMA制度は、苦情申立人が第三者の保険会社に対して行う請求を対象としています。

paragraphs.text: Since 13 May 2003, the Barometer of Liability Agreement (BOLA) has replaced the Knock-For-Knock Agreement (KFKA) to speed up motor accident claims processing among insurers. Going online from 1 May 2012, the BOLA centralised system simplifies the workflow of the subrogation recovery process among motor insurers and allows for a better management of claims settlement, and recoveries that are more efficient. However, BOLA does not diminish the right to contest liability under the law.
paragraphs.textJP: 2003年5月13日以降、過失割合協定（BOLA）は、保険者間の自動車事故保険金請求処理を迅速化するために、ノック・フォー・ノック協定（KFKA）に代わるものとして導入されました。2012年5月1日にオンライン化されたBOLA集中システムは、自動車保険者間の代位求償回収プロセスのワークフローを簡素化し、保険金精算のより良い管理とより効率的な回収を可能にしています。ただし、BOLAは法律の下で賠償責任を争う権利を減じるものではありません。

paragraphs.text: Insurers use BOLA to determine how much each involved party is liable in the event of a motor accident, through the use of predetermined charts of various motor accident claims scenarios in which the apportionment of liability has been agreed by all motor insurers in Singapore. If the motor accident occurs within the scenarios as listed, the insurers will settle the subrogation claim among themselves in accordance with the predetermined apportionment of liability.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者はBOLAを使用して、自動車事故における各関係当事者の賠償責任の度合いを決定します。これは、シンガポールのすべての自動車保険者が賠償責任の配分に合意したさまざまな自動車事故請求シナリオの所定の図表を使用して行われます。自動車事故が一覧に記載されたシナリオ内で発生した場合、保険者は所定の賠償責任配分に従って保険者間で代位求償請求を精算します。

paragraphs.text: Although BOLA does not stop an insurer from exercising its subrogation rights, it helps to reduce and resolve disputes to reach amicable settlement among insurers, pertaining to the liability of their insured drivers in any given circumstances. Thus, this leads to very few cases in which the insurers need to resort to litigation to seek subrogation recovery.
paragraphs.textJP: BOLAは保険者の代位求償権の行使を妨げるものではありませんが、いかなる状況においても被保険運転者の賠償責任に関する保険者間の紛争を削減・解決し、友好的な和解に達することに資します。その結果、保険者が代位求償回収のために訴訟に訴える必要のある事案は極めて少なくなっています。

paragraphs.text: No Claim Discount (NCD) may not be affected if driver is found totally not at fault in an accident involving another vehicle. In all other cases, the NCD may be affected.
paragraphs.textJP: 他の車両が関与する事故において運転者が100%無過失と認定された場合、無事故割引（NCD）は影響を受けない場合があります。その他のすべての場合、NCDは影響を受ける可能性があります。

paragraphs.text: The State Courts of Singapore has published the “Motor Accident Guide” (MAG) in 2014, which serves a reference to the guidelines that the courts use to assess liability in different scenarios commonly encountered in road traffic accidents. This guide helps motor insurers, lawyers, and road users to assess the likely level of liability in any given road traffic accident scenarios using clearly illustrated diagrams and pictures followed by simple text. In this manner, the MAG will improve the negotiation between parties attempting to settle personal injury or property damage claims arising from the road traffic accidents. For more details on these guidelines, please refer to a copy of the MAG.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポール国家裁判所は2014年に「自動車事故ガイド」（MAG）を公表しました。これは、道路交通事故で一般的に遭遇するさまざまなシナリオにおいて裁判所が賠償責任を評価するために使用するガイドラインの参照として機能します。このガイドは、自動車保険者、弁護士および道路利用者が、明確な図解と簡潔なテキストを使用して、あらゆる交通事故シナリオにおける賠償責任の見込まれる水準を評価するのに役立ちます。このようにして、MAGは道路交通事故から生じる人身傷害や財物損害の請求の和解を試みる当事者間の交渉を改善します。これらのガイドラインの詳細については、MAGのコピーを参照してください。

subsections.label: E1
subsections.title: Motor Accident Claims Online (Maco)
subsections.titleJP: 自動車事故請求オンライン（Maco）

paragraphs.text: In March 2022, it was reported that an online traffic accident claims simulator had been developed by the Singapore Courts and the Singapore Academy of Law. The simulator is called the Motor Accident Claims Online (Maco) which generates approximate figures for claimants very quickly through the application of technology that considers current laws and case precedents.
paragraphs.textJP: 2022年3月、シンガポール裁判所とシンガポール法律アカデミーがオンライン交通事故請求シミュレーターを開発したことが報告されました。このシミュレーターはMotor Accident Claims Online（Maco）と呼ばれ、現行の法律と判例を考慮した技術の適用により、請求者に対して概算の数字を非常に迅速に生成します。

paragraphs.text: Motorists involved in an accident are now able to have a good idea of the amount of damages they can claim from a legal perspective in a matter of minutes, by merely answering a series of multiple-choice questions. Maco can determine how liable a person is, depending on where the accident took place and the relative positions of the two vehicles, and how much a victim suffering from a specific type of injury can claim.
paragraphs.textJP: 事故に関与した運転者は、一連の選択式の質問に回答するだけで、数分以内に法的観点から請求可能な損害賠償額のおおよその見通しを得ることができるようになりました。Macoは、事故が発生した場所と2台の車両の相対的な位置に応じて当事者の過失割合を判定し、特定の種類の傷害を被った被害者が請求できる金額を算出できます。

paragraphs.text: This will enable parties involved in motor accidents to decide on the viability of pursuing legal action against each other or insurers when there are differing accounts or evidence offered by the parties concerned, or if the parties are not happy with the compensation offered by the insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: これにより、自動車事故の関係者は、関係当事者から異なる説明や証拠が提示された場合や、保険者が提示する補償額に不満がある場合に、互いにまたは保険者に対して法的措置を追求する実行可能性を判断できます。

paragraphs.text: Maco can enable parties to reach an out of court settlement thereby avoiding the need for legal proceedings. In this manner, time and court resources can be better used. It is envisaged that this simulator will help manage motorists' expectations of their legal position or exposure and allow them to decide next steps accordingly.
paragraphs.textJP: Macoは当事者が裁判外で和解に達することを可能にし、法的手続きの必要性を回避します。これにより、時間と裁判所のリソースをより有効に活用できます。このシミュレーターは、運転者が自らの法的立場やリスクの期待値を管理し、それに応じた次のステップを決定することを支援すると期待されています。

paragraphs.text: Maco can be accessed at https://motoraccidents.lawnet.sg/
paragraphs.textJP: Macoは https://motoraccidents.lawnet.sg/ でアクセスできます。

paragraphs.text: Maco is currently non-binding and is not meant as a perfect substitute for legal advice. At this moment, it cannot consider non-injury claims such as loss of income or vehicle or property damage. Maco is part of the industry's plans in having low-level motor accidents entirely adjudicated by artificial intelligence (AI).
paragraphs.textJP: Macoは現時点では拘束力を持たず、法的助言の完全な代替を意図するものではありません。現時点では、所得損失や車両・財物損害などの非傷害請求を考慮することはできません。Macoは、軽微な自動車事故を完全に人工知能（AI）により裁定するという業界の計画の一環です。

paragraphs.text: Many of the considerations and requirements for Private Motorcycle Insurance are similar to those for Private Motor Car Insurance. Both are subject to the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960 and the issue of Certificates of Insurance. Many of the Motorcycle Insurance terms of coverage, exclusions and conditions are similar to those of Private Motor Car Insurance policies.
paragraphs.textJP: 自家用二輪車保険の多くの考慮事項と要件は、自家用自動車保険と同様です。両者とも自動車（第三者リスクおよび補償）法1960年および保険証明書の発行の対象となります。二輪車保険の補償条件、免責事由および条件の多くは、自家用自動車保険と類似しています。

paragraphs.text:
The insurance market for motorcycles, however, is comparatively limited owing to some aspects of Private Motorcycle Insurance, which are unique to that class of vehicles, such as the following:
▪ There are many more young drivers owning or riding motorcycles than private cars; and
▪ The risk of a pillion rider suffering a serious injury in an accident.
paragraphs.textJP:
しかし、二輪車の保険市場は比較的限定的です。これは、自家用二輪車保険の以下のような当該車種に固有の側面に起因します：
▪ 自家用車よりも二輪車を所有または運転する若年ドライバーがはるかに多い
▪ タンデムライダーが事故で重傷を負うリスク

paragraphs.text: Intended for two-wheeled (and scooter-type three-wheeled) vehicles, Private Motorcycle Insurance policies are similar to Private Motor Car Insurance policies, but covers “Loss Of Or Damage To The Insured Vehicle” and “Liability To Third Party” only (see sample Motorcycle policy in Appendix 1D).
paragraphs.textJP: 二輪車（およびスクータータイプの三輪車）を対象とした自家用二輪車保険は、自家用自動車保険と類似していますが、「被保険車両の滅失・損害」および「第三者賠償責任」のみを補償します（見本は付録1Dの二輪車保険を参照）。

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Section I - Loss Of Or Damage To The Insured Vehicle
subsections.titleJP: 第I部 — 被保険車両の滅失・損害

paragraphs.text: This is similar to Section I of the Private Motor Car Insurance, but with one significant difference – the loss of or damage to accessories and spare parts by burglary, housebreaking or theft is not covered, unless the insured motorcycle is stolen at the same time.
paragraphs.textJP: これは自家用自動車保険の第I部と類似していますが、一つの重要な違いがあります——付属品やスペアパーツの侵入窃盗や盗難による滅失・損害は、被保険二輪車が同時に盗難されない限り補償されません。

paragraphs.text: If the motorcycle is stolen outside of Singapore, some insurers will pay a lower amount, such as 50% of the market value of the motorcycle up to S$2,500.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポール国外で二輪車が盗難にあった場合、一部の保険者は支払額を減額し、二輪車の市場価値の50%を上限としてS$2,500までの低い金額を支払います。

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: Section II - Liability To Third Parties
subsections.titleJP: 第II部 — 第三者賠償責任

paragraphs.text: As with Private Motor Car Insurance, the insurer will indemnify the insured or the named driver for unlimited legal liability arising from death of or bodily injury to a third party (including pillion rider), as well as damage to property of a third party. The property damage limit is much lower than that of Private Motor Car Insurance, e.g. usually up to S$500,000 for any one claim or series of claims arising out of any one event. However, unlike Private Motor Car Insurance, this section does not cover the insured when he is driving another motorcycle.
paragraphs.textJP: 自家用自動車保険と同様に、保険者は被保険者または記名運転者が第三者（タンデムライダーを含む）の死亡または身体傷害から生じる無制限の法的賠償責任についててん補します。第三者の財物損害に対する賠償責任も補償されます。財物損害の限度額は自家用自動車保険よりもかなり低く、通常、1事故または1事象から生じる一連の請求につきS$500,000（50万シンガポールドル）までです。ただし、自家用自動車保険とは異なり、このセクションは被保険者が別の二輪車を運転している場合を補償しません。

paragraphs.text: The standard Private Motorcycle Insurance policy protects the insured while using the motorcycle for social, domestic and pleasure purposes and in connection with the insured’s business only. It excludes property damage or bodily injury arising from the use of the vehicle for hire, reward, tuition, driving test, racing, pace-making, reliability test or speed-testing, carriage of goods (other than samples) in connection with any trade or business, and any purpose in connection with the motor trade. Appendix 1A ABC INSURANCE COMPANY (SINGAPORE) LIMITED (Registration No: 2009-12345-A) 21 Any Street, ABC Centre, Singapore 654321 Tel: (65) 6789 8181 Fax: (65) 6789 8282 E-mail: contactus@abcinco.com.sg Website: www.abcinco.com.sg PROPOSAL FORM FOR MOTOR INSURANCE STATEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 23(5) OF INSURANCE ACT 1966 (OR ANY SUBSEQUENT AMENDMENTS THEREOF) You are to disclose in this proposal form, fully and faithfully all the facts which you know in respect of the risk that is being proposed, otherwise the policy issued hereunder may be void. Name NRIC/Passport No. Sex Male Female Date of Birth Registration Date of Driving Licence Occupation Indoor Outdoor Relationship to registered owner DETAILS OF INSURANCE COVER Period: From to No Claim Discount entitlement % To enable us to confirm your NCD entitlement, please provide the details below: Previous Insurer Policy No. Vehicle No. Expiry Date I undertake to pay any difference in the premium payable under the policy issued by ABC if my previous insurer state that I am not entitled to a No Claim Discount or that my NCD entitlement is lower than what is stated here. FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY Agent/Representative: Code No.: Policy No.: Date: Premium: Excess: Entry by: Date: Checked by: Date: Policy Delivery: HAND/MAIL Remarks: Appendix 1B PRIVATE MOTOR CAR INSURANCE POLICY TERMS AND CONDITIONS This Policy ("Policy") sets out the terms, conditions, limitations, and exclusions of this Policy between ABC Insurance Company (Singapore) Limited and you. Please read this Policy carefully as it is a legal document. Your Application Form submitted to us, any declarations made by you, the Certificate of Insurance and any Endorsement(s) issued by us in respect of this Policy shall form part of this Policy. In consideration of the full payment of premium received and accepted by us, we will provide the cover listed in this Policy to the extent of the type of cover you have chosen and which is specified in the Schedule. The due observance and fulfilment of the terms of this Policy in so far as they relate to anything to be done or complied with by you and the truth of the statements and answers in the Application Form made by you shall be a condition precedent to any liability on our part. SCOPE OF COVER SECTION I – INSURANCE ON THE MOTOR VEHICLE 1. (a) We will indemnify you against loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle and its Accessories and parts whilst thereon caused by: (i) accidental collision or overturning (ii) fire, external explosion, self-ignition, lightning, burglary, housebreaking or theft (iii) malicious act (iv) strike, riot or civil commotion (v) flood, typhoon, hurricane, volcanic eruption, earthquake or other convulsion of nature. (b) Our maximum liability shall be limited to the prevailing Market Value of the Insured Vehicle at the time of the loss or damage. 2. (a) We will at our option repair, reinstate, replace or make a cash settlement for the loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle or its Accessories or parts and our liability shall not exceed the Market Value of the Insured Vehicle or its Accessories or parts lost or damaged and the reasonable cost of fitting such parts. (b) In the event that we elect to repair the Insured Vehicle, we shall: (i) be entitled to have the repairs carried out at a workshop selected by us or (ii) allow the repairs to be carried out at the manufacturer's appointed workshop if the Insured Vehicle is still under the manufacturer's warranty and if we consider it necessary that specialised skills or tools be used to carry out the repairs. (c) It is a condition precedent to our liability under this Section provided: (i) we are allowed to examine the nature and extent of the loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle prior to its repairs (ii) you have not made any prior attempt to recover from any other person (iii) we agree to repair, reinstate, replace any part of the damage or loss to the Insured Vehicle or its Accessories or parts. (d) If the Insured Vehicle is the subject of a hire purchase or leasing agreement any cash settlement will be paid to the legal owner of the Insured Vehicle named in the Schedule. 3. (a) If the Insured Vehicle is damaged in an accident, we will pay up to S$200 the reasonable cost of moving it to the nearest repairer. We also reserve the right to arrange for the damaged Insured Vehicle to be repaired at another workshop. (b) If the Insured Vehicle is damaged in an accident, you may authorise the repair provided: (i) the estimated cost of the repair is not more than S$300 after deduction of the Excess as stated in the Certificate of Insurance when you made a claim under Section I of this Policy and (ii) you forward us the detailed estimated cost of repair without delay. 4. (a) We will not pay for: (i) daily transport allowance or any losses due to unavailability of Accessories and/or parts (ii) depreciation, wear and tear, mechanical or electrical breakdowns, failures or breakages of the Insured Vehicle (iii) damage to tyres unless the Insured Vehicle suffers other damage at the same time (iv) loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle whilst in transit (including loading and unloading incidental to such transit) except where such transit is between West Malaysia and Penang; Changi Point, Singapore and Tanjong Berlungkor, Johore (v) Excess and Additional Excess inclusive of GST if applicable (vi) the Unnamed Driver Excess inclusive of GST if applicable. The Unnamed Driver Excess is to be applied as follows: The Unnamed Driver Excess applicable Either age 26 years old and below or has less than one year driving experience S$2,500 Age 27 years old and above S$500 (b) Notwithstanding 4(a) above, the Excess, Additional Excess or Unnamed Driver Excess will not apply to any loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle caused by fire, external explosion, self-ignition, lightning, burglary, housebreaking or theft. 5. We will pay the cost of replacing any broken windscreen or window glass on the Insured Vehicle provided that: (i) there is no other damage to the Insured Vehicle (ii) the repairs are carried out at the windscreen repairer appointed or approved by us (iii) you are to notify us prior to any repairs or for any replacement of your windscreen or window glass. Notwithstanding the coverage provided above, we will not pay for: (a) the amount specified as Windscreen Excess in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance (b) the cost of repairs for chipped or scratched windscreen or window glass on the Insured Vehicle which does not require a replacement (c) for transport allowance or any losses due to unavailability of parts. This payment shall not be construed as a claim for purposes under Conditions Clause 10 (No Claim Discount) of this Policy. SECTION II - LIABILITY TO THIRD PARTIES 1. We will indemnify you against legal liability arising from any accident involving the Insured Vehicle in respect of: (a) death of or bodily injury to any person (b) damage to property up to S$5,000,000 in respect of any one claim or series of claims arising out of any one event. 2. We will also indemnify against the same liability: (a) whilst you are driving any other car not belonging or hired to you, your employer or partner, provided that the said car is not covered by any other insurance for any loss or liability (b) any Person or Classes of Persons entitled to drive under your current Certificate of Insurance whilst driving the Insured Vehicle, provided that such Person or Classes of Persons is not entitled to indemnity under any other policy (c) any passenger mounting into, dismounting from or travelling in the Insured Vehicle. 3. We will pay all costs and expenses incurred with our written consent in connection with any claim under this Section. 4. This Section does not insure legal liability for: (a) death of or bodily injury to an employee arising out of and in the course of employment by anyone indemnified under this Section (b) loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle or any property owned by or in the care of anyone indemnified under this Section (c) any Act of Terrorism. SECTION III - MEDICAL EXPENSES We will pay you, your authorised driver, named driver or passenger up to S$300 for reasonable medical expenses incurred by each person who is injured whilst in the Insured Vehicle as a direct and immediate result of an accident involving the Insured Vehicle. The benefit is payable under this Section provided the number of persons in the Insured Vehicle at the time of the accident does not exceed its seating capacity. SECTION IV - PERSONAL ACCIDENT BENEFITS 1. We will pay: (a) you or your personal representative the amount stated in the Scale of Compensation for death or bodily injury arising out of an accident while you are the driver of or a passenger in the Insured Vehicle or any private car If you have more than one private car policy with us, payment will be made under one Policy only. (b) each of the passengers and authorised driver in the Insured Vehicle or their personal representatives half of the amount stated in the Scale of Compensation for death or bodily injury arising out of an accident involving the Insured Vehicle. 2. We will not pay for death or bodily injury: (a) arising from intentional self-injury, suicide, attempted suicide, physical or mental defect or infirmity (b) arising from any accident happening whilst the person who is claiming compensation under this Section is under the influence of intoxicating liquor or drugs (c) if the number of persons (including the driver) in the Insured Vehicle at the time of accident exceeds the seating capacity stated in the Schedule. SCALE OF COMPENSATION (i) Death S$20,000 (ii) Total and irrecoverable loss of all sight in both eyes S$20,000 (iii) Total loss by physical severance at or above the wrist or ankle of both hands or S$20,000 both feet or of one hand together with one foot (iv) Total loss by physical severance at or above the wrist or ankle of one hand or S$20,000 one foot together with the total and irrecoverable loss of all sight in one eye (v) Total and irrecoverable loss of all sight in one eye S$10,000 (vi) Total loss by physical severance at or above the wrist or ankle of one hand or S$10,000 one foot We will make payment under only one of the sub-sections (i) to (vi) above in respect of any occurrence and in the event of multiple injuries sustained, we will pay for the event that gives the highest benefit. Our total liability in the aggregate shall not exceed S$20,000 during any one period of insurance. GENERAL EXCLUSIONS 1. Driving And Use This Policy does not insure any accident, loss, damage, injury or liability where any motor vehicle in respect of which this Policy provides indemnity is: (a) being used outside the Limitations as to Use as stated in the Certificate of Insurance (b) being driven by anyone other than the Person or Classes of Persons entitled to drive under your Certificate of Insurance (c) being driven by anyone whilst under the influence of intoxicating liquor or drugs (d) being used or driven when it is not registered under the Road Traffic Act 1961 or when its registration under the Road Traffic Act 1961 has been cancelled under the relevant provisions as amended from time to time (e) being used or driven with modifications that have not been approved by the Registrar of Vehicles in accordance with the Road Traffic (Motor Vehicles, Registration and Licensing) Rules or by any relevant regulatory authority. 2. Contractual Liability This Policy does not insure any liability which attaches because of an agreement which you have made with any other party but which would not have attached in the absence of such agreement. 3. War And Civil War Risks This Policy does not cover any liability for loss or damage directly or indirectly occasioned by, happening through or in consequence of: (a) war, invasion, act of foreign enemy, hostilities or war-like operations (whether war be declared or not), civil war (b) mutiny, civil commotion assuming the proportions of or amounting to a popular rising, military rising, insurrection, rebellion, revolution, military or usurped power, or any act of any person or persons acting on behalf of or in connection with any organisation the objects of which are to include the overthrowing or influencing of any de jure or de facto government by terrorism or by any violent means. 4. Nuclear Risks This Policy does not insure loss, damage, injury or death caused by or arising from radioactivity or from the use, existence or escape of any nuclear fuel, material or waste. 5. Terrorism This Policy does not cover any liability for loss or damage directly or indirectly occasioned by, happening through or in consequence of any Act of Terrorism. This also excludes loss, damage, cost or expense of whatsoever nature directly or indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any action taken in controlling, preventing, suppressing or in any way relating to any Act of Terrorism. We will not pay for any losses directly or indirectly arising out of, contributed to or caused by, or resulting from or in connection with any act of Nuclear, Chemical or Biological terrorism regardless of any other cause or event contributing at the same time as or in any other sequence to the loss. If we allege that by reason of this exclusion, any loss damage, cost or expense is not covered by this insurance the burden of proving the contrary shall be upon you. 6. Jurisdiction We will not be bound by judgements not in the first instance delivered by or obtained from a court within West Malaysia or the Republic of Singapore. CONDITIONS 1. Observance Of Terms It is a condition precedent to our liability that anyone claiming indemnity or benefit complies with the terms of this Policy. 2. Geographical Limits This Policy provides cover for any accident, loss, damage, injury or liability arising within the following geographical areas: (i) the Republic of Singapore (ii) West Malaysia (iii) that part of Thailand within 80km of the border between Thailand and West Malaysia. 3. Precautions You must take all reasonable precautions to reduce or remove the risk of damage, loss or injury. In the event of any accident or breakdown to the Insured Vehicle, it shall not be left unattended unless all necessary and proper precautions are taken by you to prevent any further loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle or to any third party. We shall also not be liable for any loss or damage caused to the Insured Vehicle arising from its use prior to all necessary repairs being effected. 4. Maintenance Of Insured Vehicle You must maintain the Insured Vehicle in an efficient and roadworthy condition. We shall have at all reasonable times free and full access to examine the Insured Vehicle or any part thereof. 5. Notification Of Accident (a) If the Insured Vehicle is lost, damaged or involved in an accident, irrespective of whether it would give rise to a claim, you must: (i) within 24 hours thereafter or latest by the next working day, report the accident, loss or damage to us and make available the Insured Vehicle for inspection at an Accident Reporting Centre, whether or not the Insured Vehicle has suffered any visible damage and whether or not you intend to claim under this Policy or claim against any other party (ii) notify us immediately of any claim by a third party or any writ, summons, offer of composition or notice of any other proceedings received by you (iii) not admit liability or make any offer or payment without our written consent and (iv) report to the Police and co-operate with us in securing the conviction of the offender in the case of theft or other criminal act that may give rise to a claim under this Policy. (b) If you fail to comply with above Condition 5(a)(i) of this Policy, your No Claim Discount (NCD) will be reduced in accordance with Condition 10(iii) of this Policy. 6. Conduct Of Proceedings We are entitled: (i) to conduct all proceedings relating to a claim, summons, criminal prosecution, inquest or any other inquiries (ii) to pursue in your name for our benefit any claim for indemnity or damages and you shall give us all such information and assistance as we may reasonably require. 7. Other Insurance If any other insurance covers the same damage loss or liability we will pay only our rateable proportion of any claim. 8. False Or Exaggerated Claims No payment will be made under this Policy if the claim is: (i) in any respect fraudulent (ii) intentionally exaggerated (iii) supported by false declaration (iv) due to loss or damage intentionally caused by you or a person acting with your express or implied consent (v) due to an act committed in violation of any law. 9. Cancellation (a) We may cancel this Policy by giving you seven days' notice at your last known address and you must return your original Certificate of Insurance to us. You may also cancel this Policy by notifying us in writing or through the phone and returning your original Certificate of Insurance to us, and the cancellation will take effect from the date we receive your original Certificate of Insurance. Upon the cancellation of this Policy and subject to Clause 9(b) below, any refund of premium will be calculated as follows: If you take up another insurance policy with us within 90 days from the effective date of cancellation of this Policy Premium Refund = Premium X Unexpired Period of Insurance (days) Original Period of Insurance (days) If you do not take up another motor insurance policy with us Premium Refund = (85% of Premium) X Unexpired Period of Insurance (days) Original Period of Insurance (days) Any premium below the minimum sum of S$25 (subject to GST) upon cancellation is not refundable. This minimum sum will also be imposed if the Policy is not taken up after issuance. (b) You will not receive any refund of premium if: (i) any claim has been made or has arisen under this Policy; or (ii) we have paid one or more claims under or in connection with this Policy. 10. No Claim Discount (NCD) (i) If no claims are made under this Policy, we will reduce your premiums payable during renewal according to the following scale: Period of Insurance Discount The preceding year 10% The preceding two consecutive years 20% The preceding three consecutive years 30% The preceding four consecutive years 40% The preceding five consecutive years 50% (ii) If at the time of a claim the NCD is 40% or 50%, the NCD shall be reduced to 10% or 20% respectively at the next renewal of the Policy. If the NCD is 30% or less, there will be no NCD at the next renewal of the Policy (iii) Notwithstanding the above, in the event you fail to comply with the Notification of Accident pursuant to Condition 5(a)(i) set out above, your NCD will be further reduced by an additional 10% as shown in the following table: Existing NCD Non-Reporting NCD (this is your resulting NCD after such 10% reduction) 50% 40% 40% 30% 30% 20% 20% 10% 10% 0% 0% 0% 11. Governing Laws This Policy is governed by the laws of Singapore and the Road Transport Act 1987 of Malaysia and any subsequent revisions of the legislations thereof. 12. Avoidance Of Certain Terms And Right Of Recovery In the event, we are obliged to pay by virtue of the following agreements: (a) Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960 (b) the Agreement executed between the Minister of Transport of the Government of Malaysia and the Motor Insurers' Bureau of West Malaysia on 30 March 1992 (c) the Agreement executed between the Minister of Finance of the Republic of Singapore and the Motor Insurers' Bureau of Singapore on the 22 February 1975 (d) and any subsequent revisions to the legislation or agreement thereof which we would not otherwise be liable under this Policy, you or your authorised driver shall repay the amount to us. 13. Exclusion Of Third Party Rights A person who is not party to this agreement shall have no right under the Contracts (Right of Third Parties) Act 2001 to enforce any of its terms. 14. Payment Before Cover Warranty (a) The premium due must be paid to the insurer (or the intermediary through whom this Policy was effected) on or before the inception date (“the inception date”) or the renewal date of the coverage. Payment shall deemed to have been effected to the Insurer or the intermediary when one of the following acts takes place: (i) Cash or honoured cheque for the premium is handed over to the Insurer or the intermediary; (ii) A credit or debit card transaction for the premium is approved by the issuing bank. (iii) A payment through an electronic medium including the internet is approved by the relevant party. (iv) A credit in favour of the insurer or the intermediary is made through an electronic medium including the internet. (b) In the event that that the total premium due is not paid and actually received in full by the Insurer (or the intermediary through whom this Policy was effected) on or before the inception date or renewal date, then the insurance shall not attach and no benefits whatsoever shall be payable by the insurer. Any payment received thereafter shall be of no effect whatsoever as cover has not attached. 15. Arbitration Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, including any question regarding its existence, validity or termination, shall be referred to and finally resolved by arbitration in Singapore in accordance with the Arbitration Rules of the Singapore International Arbitration Centre for the time being in force, which rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference in this Arbitration Clause. DEFINITION OF TERMS IN POLICY Any word or expression which has a specific meaning should have this meaning attached to the word or expression found in the Policy, Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance and any Endorsement(s). Accessories refer to all audio, video and other like equipment fitted into the car by the manufacturer or distributor. These standard fittings are covered under the Policy. Any future additions will not be covered unless declared and notified to us. There may be additional premium to be paid for subsequently fitted accessories. Act of Terrorism refers to an act, including but not limited to use of force or violence and/or threat, of any person or group(s) of persons, whether acting alone or on behalf of or in connection with any organisation(s) or government(s), committed for political, religious, ideological or similar purposes including the intention to influence any government and/or to put the public or any section of the public, in fear. Additional Excess refers to the amount that you have to pay in addition to the Excess amount stated in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance when you make a claim on your Policy. Application Form refers to the completed application form signed by you for a motor insurance policy. Biological Agent refers to any pathogenic (disease producing) micro-organism(s) and/or biologically produced toxin(s) including genetically modified and chemically synthesised toxins which cause illness and/or death in humans, animals or plants. Chemical Agent refers to any compound which, when suitably disseminated, produces incapacitating, damaging or lethal effects on people, animals, plants or material property. Endorsement refers to an amendment to the Policy that becomes part of the document. The Endorsement which applies to your Policy is indicated in the Schedule and/or the Certificate of Insurance. Excess refers to the amount shown in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance which you shall bear in respect of each and every accident. Insured Vehicle refers to the vehicle which is described in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance. Market Value refers to the cost of replacing the Insured Vehicle with one of the same make and model, of similar condition and age as prevailing immediately prior to the date of the loss or accident. Named Driver refers to the driver(s) named in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance who will enjoy the same Excess as you. Authorised drivers not named in the Schedule are subject to an Unnamed Driver Excess as stated in the Policy. No Claim Discount (NCD) refers to the discount percentage given to you each policy year where no claim is made against the Policy in the preceding year. Non-Reporting NCD refers to the loss of No Claim Discount as a result of not reporting or late reporting of an accident. Nuclear, Chemical or Biological Terrorism refers to the use of any nuclear weapon or device or the emission, discharge, dispersal, release or escape of any solid, liquid, gaseous Chemical Agent and/or Biological Agent in an Act of Terrorism. Period of Insurance refers to the Period of Insurance which the Insured Vehicle is insured under this Policy as stated in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance. Preferred Workshop refers to a workshop of your choice where you can repair the Insured Vehicle in the event of an accident damage for which a claim is to be made. Policy refers to this Policy, your Application Form submitted to us, any declarations made by the Insured Persons, the Schedule, the Certificate of Insurance and any Endorsement(s) issued by us in respect of this Policy. Policyholder refers to the person named in the Certificate of Insurance and under whose name this Policy has been issued. Primary Driver refers to the owner of the Insured Vehicle as stated in the Schedule. If the owner is not licensed to drive, the person designated as the main driver will be deemed the Primary Driver. Unnamed Driver refers to any person who is not named in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance but is authorised by the Policyholder to drive the Insured Vehicle. Unnamed Driver Excess refers to the excess that you have to pay in addition to the Excess and Additional Excess, if applicable, as stated in the Schedule and/or Certificate of Insurance when you make a claim on your Policy. We, we, Our, our, Us, us or ABC Insurance refers to ABC Insurance Company (Singapore) Limited. You, you, Yours or yours refers to the Policyholder. ENDORSEMENTS EACH OF THE FOLLOWING ENDORSEMENTS IS OPERATIVE ONLY WHEN THE ENDORSEMENT NUMBER IS SHOWN IN THE SCHEDULE UNDER THE HEADING "ENDORSEMENT OPERATIVE". M1 THIRD PARTY COVER We are only liable under Section II of the Policy. Sections I, III and IV of the Policy are not operative. M2 THIRD PARTY FIRE AND THEFT COVER We are only liable under Section I of the Policy in respect of loss or damage by fire or theft of the Insured Vehicle. We are also liable under Section II of the Policy. Sections III and IV of the Policy are not operative. M3 COE/PARF VALUE COVER In the event of an indemnity for theft or total loss, we are not liable for the residual value of the COE established PARF value of the Insured Vehicle at the time of loss. M4 NO CLAIM DISCOUNT PROTECTION Notwithstanding Condition 10(ii) of this Policy, your 50% No Claim Discount is protected as follows: Number of claim(s) made or arisen during the current Period of Insurance No Claims Discount (NCD) on renewal One 50% Two 20% Three or more 0% In the event that you fail to comply with Condition 5(a)(i) of this Policy, this No Claim Discount Protection does not protect your NCD from being reduced pursuant to Condition 10(iii) of this Policy. M5 WAIVER OF EXCESS AND DAILY TRANSPORT ALLOWANCE BENEFIT 1. In the event that we select to repair the Insured Vehicle as provided for under Section I, we will waive the Excess payable by you for each of your first two claims submitted during the Period of Insurance. 2. If the Insured Vehicle is driven by an Unnamed Driver at the date of the occurrence of the damage or loss we will only waive the amount specified as Excess in the Schedule, and not the Additional Excess and/or the Unnamed Driver Excess and/or Windscreen Excess provided for under Section I of the Policy. 3. If the period of repairs exceeds 3 days, we will pay you a daily transport allowance of S$50 up to a maximum of 7 days, for each of your first two claims submitted during the Period of Insurance. However no daily transport allowance will be paid: (a) for the first working day of the repairs (b) for a windscreen claim (c) for a total loss claim (d) when the repairs to the Insured Vehicle is delayed due to unavailability of replacement parts. 4. You undertake and agree to: (a) assign absolutely to us all rights which you may have against any third party arising from any loss or damage to your Insured Vehicle caused by accident or collision (b) irrevocably appoint us, our agents or any persons authorised by us to be your lawful Attorney and in your name and on behalf to do all or any acts and things necessary for the above purpose. Paragraph 5 of Section I is not operative. M6 GEOGRAPHICAL LIMIT Section I Clause 4(a)(iv) is amended to read as "Loss or damage whilst in transit (including loading and unloading incidental to such transit)". Condition Clause 2 is amended to read as "We do not pay for any accident, loss, damage, injury or liability arising outside the Island of Singapore". M7 VEHICLE REPAIR AT OWNER'S PREFERRED WORKSHOP Clauses 2(b) and 5(ii) of Section I of the Policy are not applicable. Signature of Policyholder Date Name of Policyholder (Company Stamp, where applicable) Signature of Driver Date Name of Driver 9. Signatures are not necessary if the completed form is sent to the Company electronically by the policyholder. 10. It is further understood that the driver [if applicable] was authorized by the policyholder to drive the vehicle. THE COMPANY DOES NOT ADMIT ANY LIABILITY BY THE ISSUE OF THIS FORM Appendix 1D ABC INSURANCE COMPANY (SINGAPORE) LIMITED (Registration No: 2009-12345-A) 21 Any Street, ABC Centre, Singapore 654321 Tel: (65) 6789 8181 Fax: (65) 6789 8282 E-mail: contactus@abcinco.com.sg Website: www.abcinco.com.sg PRIVATE MOTORCYCLE INSURANCE POLICY SCOPE OF COVER SECTION I – LOSS OF OR DAMAGE TO THE INSURED VEHICLE 1. We will indemnify you against loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle and its accessories and parts whilst thereon caused by (a) accidental collision or overturning (b) fire, external explosion, self-ignition, lightning, burglary, housebreaking or theft (c) malicious act (d) strike, riot or civil commotion (e) flood, typhoon, hurricane, volcanic eruption, earthquake or other convulsion of nature. 2 (a) We will at our option repair, reinstate replace or make a cash settlement for the loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle or its accessories or any of its parts of not more than the market value of the Insured Vehicle at the time of loss or damage, regardless of whether you are liable for the loss or damage to the Insured vehicle. (b) In the event that we elect to repair the Insured Vehicle, we shall be entitled to have the repairs carried out at a workshop selected by us. (c) We are liable under this Section provided: (i) we are allowed to examine the nature and extent of the loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle prior to its repairs. (ii) you have not made any prior attempt to recover against any other person. (d) If the Insured vehicle is the subject of a hire purchase or leasing agreement any cash settlement will be paid to the legal owner named in the Schedule. 3. We will not pay for (a) loss of use or any other consequential loss (b) depreciation, wear and tear, mechanical or electrical breakdowns, failures or breakages (c) damage to tyres unless the Insured Vehicle suffers other damage at the same time (d) loss or damage whilst in transit (including loading and unloading incidental to such transit) except where such transit is between West Malaysia and Penang; Changi Point, Singapore and Tanjong Berlungkor, Johore (e) loss or damage to accessories or parts by burglary, housebreaking or theft unless the Insured Vehicle is stolen at the same time (f) the amount specified as Excess in the Schedule and a further Excess of $500 in addition to the Excess specified in the Schedule if the Insured Vehicle is driven by a Named Driver specified in the Schedule and Certificate of Insurance who is below 21 years of age or who has held a full motor cycle driving licence for less than 2 years. The Excess will not apply to loss or damage by fire, external explosion, self-ignition, lightning, burglary, housebreaking or theft. (g) any loss or damage exceeding $300 unless a detailed estimated cost of repair is forwarded to us without delay for approval of repair. Definition "Excess" shall mean the relevant amount specified in the Schedule which you must bear in respect of each and every claim before we indemnify you. SECTION II - LIABILITY TO THIRD PARTIES 1. We will indemnity you against legal liability arising from any accident involving the Insured Vehicle in respect of (a) death of or bodily injury to any person (b) damage to property up to a limit of $500,000 for each accident 2. We will also indemnify against the same liability (a) to Persons or Classes of Persons entitled to drive under your current Certificate of Insurance whilst driving the Insured Vehicle (b) any passenger mounting into, or dismounting from or travelling in the Insured Vehicle. Provided that the passenger (i) is not driving the Insured Vehicle or in charge of the Insured Vehicle for the purpose of driving its repairs. (ii) is not entitled to indemnity under any other policy. 3. We will pay all costs and expenses incurred with our written consent in connection with any other claim under this Section. 4. This Section does not insure legal liability for (a) death of or bodily injury to an employee arising out of and in the course of employment by anyone indemnified under this Section (b) loss of or damage to the Insured Vehicle or any property owned by or in the care of anyone indemnified under this Section (c) Any act of terrorism. Definition Act of terrorism means an act, including but not limited to use of force or violence and/or the threat thereof, of any person or group(s) of persons, whether acting alone or on behalf of or in connection with any organization(s) or government(s), committed for political, religious, ideological or similar purposes including the intention to influence any government and/or to put the public or any section of the public, in fear. GENERAL EXCLUSIONS 1. Driving and Use This Policy does not insure any accident, loss, damage, injury or liability where any motor vehicle in respect of which this Policy provides indemnity is (a) being used outside the Limitations as to Use in your current Certificate of Insurance (b) being driven by anyone other than the Person or Classes of Persons entitled to drive under your current Certificate of Insurance (c) being driven by anyone whilst under the influence of intoxicating liquor or drugs. 2. Geographical Limits This Policy does not insure any accident, loss, damage, injury or liability arising outside West Malaysia, the Republic of Singapore and that part of Thailand within 80.5km of the border between Thailand and West Malaysia. 3. Contractual Liability This Policy does not insure any liability which attaches because of an agreement which you have made with any other party but which would not have attached in the absence of such agreement. 4. War Risks This Policy does not insure any consequence, whether direct or indirect, of war, invasion, act of foreign enemies, hostilities or warlike operations (whether war be declared or not), civil war, mutiny, military or popular uprising, insurrection, rebellion, revolution, military or usurped power or martial law. If we allege that by reason of this exclusion, any loss, damage, cost or expense is not covered by this insurance the burden of proving the contrary shall be upon the Insured. 5. Nuclear Risks This Policy does not insure loss, damage injury or death caused by or arising from radioactivity or from the use, existence or escape of any nuclear fuel, material or waste. 6. Nuclear, Chemical or Biological Terrorism We will not pay for any losses directly or indirectly arising out of, contributed to or caused by, or resulting from or in connection with any act of Nuclear, Chemical or Biological terrorism regardless of any other cause or event contributing at the same time as or in any other sequence to the loss. 7. Jurisdiction We will not be bound by judgements not in the first instance delivered by or obtained from a court within West Malaysia or the Republic of Singapore. Definition "Nuclear, Chemical, Biological Terrorism" shall mean the use of any nuclear weapon or device or the emission, discharge, dispersal, release or escape of any solid, liquid, gaseous Chemical agent and/or Biological agent in an Act of Terrorism. "Chemical agent" shall mean any compound which, when suitably disseminated, produces incapacitating, damaging or lethal effects on people, animals, plants or material property. "Biological agent" shall mean any pathogenic (Disease producing) micro-organism(s) and/or biological produced toxin(s) including genetically modified and chemically synthesized toxins which cause illness and/or death in humans, animals or plants. CONDITIONS 1. Observance of Terms It is a condition precedent to our liability that anyone claiming indemnity or benefit complies with the terms of this Policy. 2. Precautions You must take all reasonable precautions to reduce or remove the risks of damage, loss or injury. In the event of any accident or breakdown to the Insured Vehicle, it shall not be left unattended unless all necessary and proper precautions are taken by you to prevent any further loss or damage to the Insured Vehicle or to any third party. We shall also not be liable for any loss or damage caused to the Insured Vehicle arising from its use prior to all necessary repairs being effected. 3. Maintenance of Insured Vehicle You must maintain the Insured Vehicle in an efficient and roadworthy condition. We shall have all reasonable times free and full access to examine the Insured Vehicle or any part thereof. 4. Notification
paragraphs.textJP:
標準的な自家用二輪車保険は、二輪車を社会的、家庭的および娯楽目的ならびに被保険者の業務に関連してのみ使用している間の被保険者を保護します。有償運送、教習、運転試験、レース、ペースメイキング、信頼性試走、速度テストは除外されます。

セクション1：二輪車の保険
包括型（Comprehensive）の場合：事故、火災、盗難等による二輪車の損害を補償。被保険車両の市場価格まで。付属品・装備品も含む（限度額あり）。
第三者・火災・盗難型（TPF&T）の場合：火災および盗難のみ補償。

セクション2：第三者賠償責任
(a) 第三者の死亡または身体傷害に対する法的賠償責任（無制限）
(b) 第三者の財物損害に対する法的賠償責任（限度額S$500,000）
(c) 訴訟費用

セクション3：医療費
事故から3ヶ月以内の外来医療費を実費てん補（1人あたりS$500まで）

セクション4：人身傷害給付
運転者および同乗者の事故死亡または永久障害に対する一時金給付

免責事由：
▪ 有効な運転免許証なしでの運転
▪ アルコール・薬物の影響下での運転
▪ 改造された二輪車
▪ シンガポール国外での事故
▪ 二輪車の自然損耗

保険条件：
1. 事故後24時間以内のIDAC検査義務
2. 第三者請求の即時通知義務
3. 書面同意なき賠償責任の承認禁止
4. 窃盗の場合の警察届出義務
5. 合理的な予防措置義務
6. NCD（無事故割引）規定
7. 超過額（Excess）の適用

付録1A〜1D：自動車保険申込書・保険証券附則・保険金請求書・示談フォームのサンプル

付録1A：自動車保険申込書
申込者詳細：氏名・NRIC・住所・職業・連絡先
車両詳細：車種・登録番号・車台番号・排気量・製造年・市場価格
運転者情報：主な運転者・指名運転者（年齢・運転経歴・事故歴）
保険歴：過去の保険会社・NCD・請求歴
告知：健康状態・法律違反歴・運転免許停止歴

付録1B：保険証券附則（サンプル）
保険証券番号・保険期間・被保険者名・車両登録番号・保険の種類・保険金額・NCD・超過額・保険料

付録1C：保険金請求書（サンプル）
事故日時・場所・状況・損害の詳細・第三者の情報・警察報告番号

付録1D：示談フォーム（サンプル）
事故当事者間の責任割合の合意書

paragraphs.text: If the Insured Vehicle is lost, damaged or involved in an accident, you must: (i) within 24 hours thereafter or latest by the next working day, report the accident, loss or damage to us and make available the Insured Vehicle for inspection at an Independent Damage Assessment Centre (IDAC), whether or not the Insured Vehicle has suffered any visible damage and whether or not you intend to claim under this Policy or claim against any other party. You are not required to have the Insured Vehicle inspected by an IDAC if only the windscreen of the Insured Vehicle is damaged; (ii) notify us immediately of any claim by a third party or any writ, summons, offer of composition or notice of any other proceedings received by you; (iii) not admit liability or make any offer or payment without our written consent; and (iv) report to the Police and co-operate with us in securing the conviction of the offender in the case of theft or other criminal act that may give rise to a claim under this Policy.
paragraphs.textJP:
被保険車両が紛失、損傷または事故に遭った場合、以下を行わなければなりません：
(i) 事故、紛失または損傷の発生後24時間以内または翌営業日の午前10時までに（いずれか早い方）、当社に報告し、被保険車両に目に見える損傷があるか否か、および本保険証券に基づく請求または他の当事者への請求を行うか否かにかかわらず、独立損害査定センター（IDAC）において被保険車両を検査に供すること。被保険車両のフロントガラスのみが損傷した場合、IDACによる検査は不要です
(ii) 第三者からの請求、または受領した令状、召喚状、和解の申出、その他の訴訟手続の通知を直ちに当社に通知すること
(iii) 当社の書面による同意なく、賠償責任を認めたり、申出や支払いを行わないこと
(iv) 本保険証券に基づく請求が生じうる窃盗またはその他の犯罪行為の場合、警察に届出を行い、犯人の有罪判決の確保に当社と協力すること

paragraphs.text: If you fail to comply with above Condition 4.1(i) of this Policy, your NCD will be reduced in accordance with Condition 9(ii) of this Policy. 5. Conduct of Proceedings We are entitled (i) to conduct all proceedings relating to a claim, summons, criminal prosecution, inquest or any other inquiries (ii) to pursue in your name for our benefit any claim for indemnity or damages and you shall give us all such information and assistance as we may reasonably require. 6. Other Insurance If any other insurance covers the same damage loss or liability we will pay only our rateable proportion of any claim. 7. False or Exaggerated Claims No payment will be made under this Policy if the claim is (i) in any respect fraudulent (ii) intentionally exaggerated (iii) supported by false declaration (iv) due to loss or damage intentionally caused by you or a person acting with your express or implied consent (v) due to an act committed in violation of any law. 8. Cancellation (i) We may cancel this Policy by giving you seven days' notice at your last known address. You may also cancel this Policy by notifying us in writing or through the phone and returning your original Certificate of Insurance to us, and the cancellation will take effect from the date we receive your original Certificate of Insurance. Upon the cancellation of this Policy and subject to Clause 8(2) below, any refund of premium will be calculated as follows: If you take up another motor insurance policy with us within 90 days from the effective date of cancellation of this Policy Premium Refund = Premium Unexpired period of insurance (days) Original period of insurance (days) If you do not take up another motor insurance policy with us Premium Refund = (85% of Premium) X Unexpired period of insurance (days) Original period of insurance (days) (ii) You will not receive any refund of premium if: (i) any claim has been made or has arisen under this Policy; or (ii) we have paid one or more claims under or in connection with this Policy. 9. No Claim Discount (i) If no claim is made under this Policy during a period of insurance of one year or more immediately preceding the renewal of this Policy, the No Claim Discount will be given on renewal according to the following scale: Period of Insurance Discount The preceding year 10% The preceding two consecutive years 15% The preceding three consecutive years 20% (ii) If at the time of a claim the No Claim Discount is 20% or less, the No Claim Discount shall be reduced to Nil at the next renewal of the Policy. (iii) If you fail to comply with Condition 4.1(i) of this Policy, your Accident NCD will be reduced as shown in the following table: Accident NCD Non-Reporting NCD (this is your resulting NCD after such reduction) 20% 15% 15% 10% 10% 0% 0% 0% 10.
