paragraphs.text: Some policies also include total paralysis, incurable insanity, injuries resulting in being permanently bedridden or being unable to perform three or more Activities of Daily Living (ADLs), following an accident to constitute permanent total disablement. ADLs are Washing, Dressing, Feeding, Toileting, Mobility, and Transferring, as specifically defined in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険証券は、全身麻痺、不治の精神障害、事故に起因する永久的な寝たきり状態、または日常生活動作（ADL）を3つ以上行えない状態も永久全身障害を構成するものとして含みます。ADLとは保険証券に具体的に定義された入浴、着衣、食事、排泄、移動および体位変換です。

paragraphs.text: In addition, some insurers will pay a monthly income benefit (e.g. S$3,000) for six months in the event of permanent total disablement as specified, provided the insured has been gainfully employed or engaged in any registered business at the time of the accident. Therefore, insurers do not normally offer this benefit to a homemaker or student.
paragraphs.textJP: さらに、一部の保険者は、事故発生時に被保険者が有報酬の雇用に就いているか、登録事業に従事していることを条件として、所定の永久全身障害の場合に6か月間の月額所得給付金（例：S$3,000）を支払います。したがって、保険者は通常、主婦や学生にはこの給付を提供しません。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers are prepared to provide benefits for the loss of parts of limbs, e.g. thumbs, fingers, and toes. The percentage of the capital sum paid for such disabilities is usually a smaller percentage of the capital sum insured than that paid for the loss of an entire limb or eyesight, and often varies according to the actual loss of the limb: for example, 20% for a thumb, 10% for a finger, and 5% for a big toe. This cover is of particular benefit to a person who relies on his hands for a profession, such as a surgeon or professional musician.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、四肢の一部（例：親指、指、足指）の喪失に対する給付を提供する用意があります。かかる障害に対して支払われる保険金額のパーセンテージは、通常、四肢全体または視力の喪失に対して支払われるパーセンテージよりも小さく、実際の四肢の喪失に応じて異なることが多いです。例えば、親指は20%、指は10%、足の親指は5%です。この補償は、外科医や専門音楽家など、手を職業に頼る人に特に有益です。

paragraphs.text: Even if the insured is not engaged in one of these professions, a policy that covers permanent disablement will be of immense benefit to the insured. The possibility of a person losing part of a limb in an accident is more likely than the loss of an entire limb or eyesight.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者がこれらの専門職に就いていなくても、永久障害を補償する保険証券は被保険者にとって非常に有益です。事故で四肢の一部を失う可能性は、四肢全体や視力を失う可能性よりも高いです。

paragraphs.text: Some PA Insurance policies cover burns, with benefit limits usually depending on the extent of damage to the total body surface area. When covered, Third Degree Burns are customarily subject to varying percentages (commonly ranging from 50% to 100% of the capital sum insured).
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の傷害保険は火傷を補償し、給付限度額は通常、全身の表面積に対する損傷の範囲に応じて設定されます。補償される場合、第3度の火傷は通常、保険金額の50%〜100%のさまざまなパーセンテージが適用されます。

paragraphs.text: Usually, disablement benefits are reflected under a “Table of Compensation”, especially so if the policy covers other permanent disablements. The table combines all permanent disablement benefits in one scale, with the compensation payable represented as a fixed percentage of the capital sum insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 通常、障害給付金は「補償額一覧表（Table of Compensation）」に反映されます。特に、保険証券がその他の永久障害も補償する場合はそうです。この一覧表はすべての永久障害給付金を1つのスケールにまとめ、支払われる補償金は保険金額の固定パーセンテージとして表されます。

paragraphs.text: The following Table 3.1 is an example of the disablement benefits offered under the “Table of Compensation”: Table 3.1: Table Of Compensation Description Of Permanent Disablement Percentage Of Capital Sum Insured (A) Permanent Total Disablement 100% (B) Loss of sight of both eyes 100% (C) Loss of two or more limbs, or all fingers and thumbs of both hands 100% (D) Loss of one limb and sight of one eye 100% (E) Loss of sight of one eye 50% (F) Loss of one limb 50% (G) Loss of speech 50% (H) Loss of hearing in both ears 50% (I) Loss of all fingers and a thumb of one hand 50% (J) Loss of all fingers of one hand 40% (K) Loss of hearing in one ear 20% (L) Loss of a thumb 20% (M) Loss of any one finger 10% (N) Loss of all toes of one foot 15% (O) Loss of a big toe 5% (P) Loss of any one other toe 2% (Q) Third degree burns (damage as a percentage of total body surface) (1) Head: (a) equal to or greater than 8% (b) equal to or greater than 5% but less than 8% (c) equal to or greater than 2% but less than 5% (2) Body: (a) equal to or greater than 20% (b) equal to or greater than 15% but less than 20% (c) equal to or greater than 10% but less than 15% 100% 75% 50% 100% 75% 50%
paragraphs.textJP:
以下の表3.1は、「補償額一覧表」に基づく障害給付金の例です：
表3.1：補償額一覧表
永久障害の種類 / 保険金額に対する割合
(A) 永久全身障害 100%
(B) 両眼の視力喪失 100%
(C) 2つ以上の四肢、または両手のすべての指と親指の喪失 100%
(D) 一肢と片眼の視力の喪失 100%
(E) 片眼の視力喪失 50%
(F) 一肢の喪失 50%
(G) 発話能力の喪失 50%
(H) 両耳の聴力喪失 50%
(I) 片手のすべての指と親指の喪失 50%
(J) 片手のすべての指の喪失 40%
(K) 片耳の聴力喪失 20%
(L) 親指の喪失 20%
(M) いずれか1本の指の喪失 10%
(N) 片足のすべての足指の喪失 15%
(O) 足の親指の喪失 5%
(P) その他のいずれか1本の足指の喪失 2%
(Q) 第3度の火傷（全身表面積に対する損傷割合）
(1) 頭部：(a) 8%以上 100% (b) 5%以上8%未満 75% (c) 2%以上5%未満 50%
(2) 身体：(a) 20%以上 100% (b) 15%以上20%未満 75% (c) 10%以上15%未満 50%

paragraphs.text: The aggregate of all percentage payable in respect of any one accident during the period of insurance (usually 12 months) shall not exceed 100% of the capital sum insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 1回の事故に関して支払われるすべてのパーセンテージの合計は、保険期間（通常12か月）中に保険金額の100%を超えないものとします。

paragraphs.text: For any disability not listed, the insurer will often compare it with the scale to find an appropriately equivalent disability and pay that benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: 一覧表に記載されていない障害については、保険者は適切に同等の障害をスケールから見つけ出し、当該給付金を支払うことが多いです。

paragraphs.text: There are insurers whose PA Insurance policies pay 100% of the capital sum insured for item (E) Loss of sight of one eye or (F) Loss of one limb. Some insurers’ policies will also pay 150% of the capital sum insured for item (A) Permanent Total Disablement.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者の傷害保険では、(E)片眼の視力喪失または(F)一肢の喪失に対して保険金額の100%を支払います。一部の保険者の保険証券は(A)永久全身障害に対して保険金額の150%も支払います。

subsections.label: D
subsections.title: Temporary Disablement (Total Or Partial)
subsections.titleJP: 一時的障害（完全・部分）

paragraphs.text: If the accident causes temporary total disablement, the insurer will pay weekly benefits to the insured, usually payable for up to 104 weeks. The amount payable is lower than the capital sum benefits payable under permanent total disablement, such as 1% of the capital sum insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 事故により一時的な完全就業不能が生じた場合、保険者は被保険者に対して週次給付金を支払います。通常、最長104週間支払われます。支払額は永久全身障害に基づく一時金給付金よりも低く、例えば保険金額の1%です。

paragraphs.text: The definition for temporary total disablement is usually less restrictive than that of permanent disablement, in that the requirement for cover to operate is that the insured is unable to perform every duty pertaining to the insured’s profession or occupation. The surgeon – who is unable to follow his profession following an injury to his hands but is capable of working as a lecturer – used in the previous example, will be entitled to benefit under temporary disablement according to the definition.
paragraphs.textJP: 一時的完全就業不能の定義は、永久障害の定義よりも制限が緩いのが通常であり、補償が適用されるための要件は、被保険者がその職業に関するすべての義務を遂行できないことです。前の例で使用した外科医——手の傷害により専門職に従事できないが講師として働くことができる者——は、この定義に基づき一時的障害の給付を受ける権利があります。

paragraphs.text: Temporary partial disability, when included, provides even lower levels of benefit than temporary total disablement (about half of the benefits) where the insured is incapable of attending to a definite part of his normal duties.
paragraphs.textJP: 一時的部分就業不能が含まれる場合、被保険者が通常の職務の明確な一部に従事できない場合に、一時的完全就業不能よりもさらに低い水準の給付金（約半分の給付金）が提供されます。

paragraphs.text: This covers reimbursement of medical expenses necessarily and reasonably incurred within a period of time (e.g. 12 months) for the treatment of bodily injuries following an accident. The cover provides a prescribed insured limit, commonly ranging from S$1,000 to S$15,000 per accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険は、事故後の身体傷害の治療のために一定期間内（例：12か月以内）に必然的かつ合理的に発生した医療費の実費てん補を補償します。補償は所定の保険限度額を提供し、一般に1事故あたりS$1,000〜S$15,000の範囲です。

paragraphs.text: Medical expenses usually refer to the costs of clinical, medical, surgical, or other remedial attention, treatment or appliances (such as crutches, wheelchairs, surgical implants, and prosthetic devices) supplied or prescribed by a qualified member of the medical profession. In addition, it includes all hospital, nursing home, and ambulance charges, including post hospitalisation care and accidental miscarriage, as well as dental treatment following an accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 医療費とは通常、資格のある医療専門家が提供または処方する臨床的、医学的、外科的またはその他の治療、処置もしくは器具（松葉杖、車椅子、外科用インプラント、義肢装具等）の費用を指します。さらに、すべての病院、介護施設および救急車の費用（退院後のケアおよび事故による流産を含む）、ならびに事故後の歯科治療も含まれます。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers separately cover up to S$2,000, following an accident, for mobility aids (such as wheelchair, crutches, ramps or railings) and ambulance services that are required following the insured’s discharge from hospital.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、事故後に退院した被保険者が必要とする移動補助具（車椅子、松葉杖、スロープ、手すり等）および救急車サービスについて、S$2,000まで別途補償します。

paragraphs.text: Others will also pay for expenses incurred for treatment by a Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) physician, acupuncturist, bonesetter, chiropractor, herbalist or physiotherapist. Cover is subject to a certain sub-limit, usually for up to 10% of the limit for medical expenses as covered by the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: また、中国伝統医学（TCM）医師、鍼灸師、接骨師、カイロプラクター、漢方医または理学療法士による治療費を支払う保険者もいます。補償は一定のサブリミットが適用され、通常、保険証券でカバーされる医療費の限度額の10%までです。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers extend the medical expenses benefit to provide for treatment of infectious diseases, such as avian influenza, dengue fever, hand foot and mouth disease, and/or certain other medical outbreaks as specified in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、鳥インフルエンザ、デング熱、手足口病、および／または保険証券に規定されたその他の特定の医療上のアウトブレイクなどの感染症の治療に対しても医療費給付を拡張しています。

paragraphs.text: Medical expenses payable are subject to the insured amount on a strict reimbursement basis. Therefore, they do form an indemnity payment, and contribution will apply if there is more than one policy covering such indemnity payment in the event of a claim for medical expenses following an accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 支払われる医療費は、保険金額を限度として厳格な実費てん補方式で支払われます。したがって、てん補の支払いを構成し、事故後の医療費の請求において当該てん補の支払いを補償する保険証券が複数ある場合、分担が適用されます。

paragraphs.text: As the name implies, some insurers will pay double the amount of the death or permanent total disablement benefit sum insured, if the accident occurs while the insured is travelling as a fare-paying passenger on public transport.
paragraphs.textJP: 名称が示すとおり、一部の保険者は、被保険者が公共交通機関に運賃を支払って乗車中に事故が発生した場合、死亡給付金または永久全身障害給付金の保険金額の2倍を支払います。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers also extend the double indemnity benefit to include any accident resulting from fire, explosion, earthquake, landslide, flood, cyclone, hurricane, typhoon, or windstorm.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、火災、爆発、地震、地滑り、洪水、サイクロン、ハリケーン、台風または暴風雨から生じる事故にも倍額てん補を拡張しています。

paragraphs.text: This benefit, when offered, provides a fixed daily benefit (e.g. S$100 per day) based on the period of hospital confinement, irrespective of the actual hospital charges, up to a certain period (e.g. 90 days). To be payable, the hospitalisation must have been a result of accidental injury as defined under the main benefit, and the commencement date of the hospital confinement must be within the policy period. Hospitalisation resulting from sickness is therefore not covered.
paragraphs.textJP: この給付は、提供される場合、入院期間に基づいて固定の日額給付金（例：1日S$100）を提供します。実際の入院費用に関係なく、一定期間（例：90日）まで支払われます。支払いが行われるためには、入院が主契約の給付で定義された偶発的傷害の結果でなければならず、入院の開始日が保険期間内でなければなりません。したがって、疾病による入院は補償されません。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers will pay double the daily benefit if the hospital confinement is in an intensive care unit for treatment of accidental injury.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、偶発的傷害の治療のために集中治療室（ICU）に入院した場合、日額給付金の倍額を支払います。

paragraphs.text: There are also insurers that extend this daily benefit to cover confinement in a hospital for treatment of one of the infectious diseases as specified in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: また、保険証券に規定された感染症の一つの治療のための入院にもこの日額給付金を拡張する保険者もいます。

paragraphs.text: In addition, some insurers will pay a recuperation benefit in one lump sum (e.g. S$500) if the confinement in a hospital, as a result of injury, exceeds a defined period, for example, 14 consecutive days.
paragraphs.textJP: さらに、一部の保険者は、傷害による入院が所定の期間（例：14日連続）を超えた場合に、一時金の療養給付金（例：S$500）を支払います。

paragraphs.text: It is common for insurers to offer the Hospital Cash/Income benefit as a standalone insurance product.
paragraphs.textJP: 入院日額／所得給付金を単独の保険商品として提供することは、保険者の間で一般的です。

paragraphs.text: Many insurers now reward insureds, who do not make any claims during the policy period, with additional sums insured at each renewal (without them having to pay additional premiums). Additional sums insured are in the form of percentages (e.g. 10%) of the original sums insured, but usually the insurer will restrict the additions to a maximum number of years (e.g. five years) or a maximum percentage (e.g. 50%).
paragraphs.textJP: 多くの保険者は、保険期間中に保険金請求を行わなかった被保険者に対し、各更新時に追加の保険金額（追加保険料の支払いなし）を報奨として提供しています。追加の保険金額は当初の保険金額のパーセンテージ（例：10%）の形式ですが、通常、保険者は追加を最大年数（例：5年）または最大パーセンテージ（例：50%）に制限します。

paragraphs.text: In place of an increase in sum insured, some insurers may offer a premium refund, usually subject to the policy having been in force with the insurer for a minimum of three consecutive years and without any claim during the policy period under review, with the “no claim premium refund” offset against the renewal premium. The refund will usually range from 5% to 25% of the premium, depending on the number of years that the policy has been in force.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金額の増額の代わりに、一部の保険者は保険料の返還を提供します。通常、保険証券が同じ保険者で最低3年連続して有効であり、審査期間中に請求がなかったことが条件です。「無事故保険料返還」は更新保険料から相殺されます。返還額は通常、保険証券が有効であった年数に応じて保険料の5%〜25%の範囲です。

sections.title: Geographical And Age Limits
sections.titleJP: 地理的範囲および年齢制限

paragraphs.text: PA Insurance cover is normally worldwide. The normal age limits range from children from 15 days old, up to adults aged 85. This is especially so if such a person is insured under an individual policy. Insurers will normally offer lower benefit limits for elderly persons (e.g. capital sum insured of S$50,000 after the age of 75 years).
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険の補償は通常、世界中で適用されます。通常の年齢制限は、生後15日の子供から85歳の成人までです。特に被保険者が個人保険に加入している場合はそうです。保険者は通常、高齢者に対してはより低い給付限度額を提供します（例：75歳以降は保険金額S$50,000）。

paragraphs.text: Many insurers now offer policies that provide PA Insurance cover for the insureds and their immediate family members, such as spouses and children.
paragraphs.textJP: 多くの保険者は、被保険者とその直系家族（配偶者および子供等）に傷害保険の補償を提供する保険を販売しています。

paragraphs.text: It makes sense to insure children. There are substantial costs involved in employing a nursing attendant to look after an injured child. The usual requirement for insuring a child is that he must be below the age of 21 years (sometimes higher, up to 25 years, if the child is still in full-time study), is unemployed, and unmarried. The minimum age varies with insurer, which can be as low as 14 days from birth.
paragraphs.textJP: 子供を付保することは合理的です。けがをした子供の看護人を雇用するには相当な費用がかかります。子供の付保の通常の要件は、21歳未満（フルタイムの就学中であれば25歳まで上げる保険者もいます）、無職、未婚であることです。最低年齢は保険者によって異なり、生後14日からの場合もあります。

paragraphs.text: The policy coverage for the family members, if included, will closely follow that of the main insured; however, the spouse and each child may receive lower benefits, for example, 25% of the insured’s capital sum insured. There is generally no limit imposed on the number of children insured under a family PA Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 家族メンバーの補償は、含まれる場合、主被保険者の補償に準じますが、配偶者および各子供はより低い給付金を受ける場合があります。例えば、被保険者の保険金額の25%です。家族傷害保険に基づき付保される子供の人数に一般的に上限はありません。

paragraphs.text: A family PA Insurance policy may also provide other cash benefits not found in a single PA Insurance policy, such as family income and child education funds in the event of accidental death of the insured, to take care of the children’s education and tuition fees. For example, some insurers will pay S$10,000 per child (with a limit of three children) in addition to the death benefit, provided that the deceased insured was gainfully employed or engaged in any registered business at the time of the accident.
paragraphs.textJP: 家族傷害保険は、個人の傷害保険にはない現金給付（被保険者の偶発的死亡の場合の家族所得や子供の教育資金等、子供の教育費や授業料を賄うためのもの）を提供する場合もあります。例えば、一部の保険者は、死亡した被保険者が事故発生時に有報酬の雇用に就いていたか登録事業に従事していたことを条件として、死亡給付金に加えて子供1人あたりS$10,000（子供3人まで）を支払います。

paragraphs.text: Not all risks of accidents are insurable. Generally, a PA Insurance policy excludes risks relating to those occurrences, which are not strictly accidental and are within the insured’s control, such as hazardous activities, or those that arise from fundamental risks, such as war and nuclear risks.
paragraphs.textJP: すべての事故リスクが付保可能なわけではありません。一般に、傷害保険は、厳密には偶発的ではなく被保険者の管理下にある事象（例：危険な活動）、または戦争や核リスクなどの基本的リスクから生じるリスクを免責とします。

一般的な免責事由は以下のとおりです：
▪ 被保険者がアルコールまたは中毒性薬物の影響下にある、もしくはこれらの影響を受けている場合（薬物の除外は、資格のある登録医が処方・指示した治療に従い服用する薬物を除く旨が記載される場合があります。ただし、薬物依存症の治療を除きます）
▪ 自傷行為、自殺、自然原因による疾病または精神障害
▪ 事故前に存在していた身体的障害
▪ 妊娠、出産（帝王切開を含む）、中絶、流産、性病、AIDS（後天性免疫不全症候群）またはAIDS関連症候群
▪ 被保険者の不必要な危険への故意の暴露（人命救助の試みを除く）
▪ 生命を脅かすまたは危険とみなされ、傷害や死亡のリスクを増大させるスポーツ
▪ 徴兵法1970年に基づく国民兵役、軍人およびNSmenの平時の予備役訓練を除く、軍隊の勤務および作戦への従事
▪ 認可された旅客輸送航空機の運賃支払い乗客としてを除く、飛行またはその他の空中活動への参加
▪ 戦争、戦争類似の作戦行為、および核リスク

交渉および保険者の検討に基づき、以下の主な拡張補償が利用可能です：
▪ 食中毒
▪ 昆虫または動物による咬傷
▪ 煙、有毒ガスによる窒息および溺死
▪ 失踪
▪ 自然の要素への暴露
▪ アマチュアスポーツへの参加
▪ 二輪車の運転またはタンデム乗車
▪ 不定期便の乗客としての旅行
▪ ハイジャック、誘拐、殺人または暴行
▪ ストライキ、暴動および内乱
▪ 核・化学・生物兵器を使用したテロ行為
▪ 緊急医療搬送およびシンガポールへの本国送還（遺体の本国送還を含む）。ただし、保険者が指名した専門会社が実施する場合に限る
▪ 救急車費用
▪ 理学療法費用
▪ 移動補助具
▪ 住宅改修
▪ 特定の感染症治療

paragraphs.text: As these extensions are only briefly mentioned here, the policy wording should be carefully checked for details of these extensions which could be subject to additional premium payments, limitations, or restrictions imposed by the insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: これらの拡張補償はここでは簡潔にのみ言及されているため、追加保険料の支払い、制限または保険者が課す制約の対象となる可能性があるこれらの拡張補償の詳細については、保険証券の文言を注意深く確認してください。

傷害保険に通常含まれる条件には以下があります：
▪ 保険金請求手続き
▪ リスクの変更
▪ 解約
▪ 条件の遵守

paragraphs.text: The insured must provide the insurer with a claim notification within a certain period, usually 30 days, although market practice may vary.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者は、一定期間内（通常30日）に保険者に対して保険金請求の通知を行わなければなりません。ただし、市場慣行は異なる場合があります。

paragraphs.text: The insured (or the legal personal representative), at his own expense, is required to furnish certificates, information, and evidence in the form as required by the insurer. In practice, the insurer issues a printed claim form (which may be downloaded from the insurer’s website), so drafted as to elicit the information that is normally required. This must be completed by the insured himself (or the claimant in the event of accidental death of the insured) and his medical attendant and returned to the insurer. Appendix 3C contains a specimen Personal Accident Claim Form.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者（またはその法定代理人）は、自己の費用負担で、保険者が要求する形式の証明書、情報および証拠を提出しなければなりません。実務上、保険者は通常必要とされる情報を引き出すように作成された印刷された保険金請求書（保険者のウェブサイトからダウンロード可能な場合もあります）を発行します。これは被保険者自身（または被保険者の偶発的死亡の場合は請求者）およびその主治医が記入し、保険者に返送しなければなりません。付録3Cに傷害保険請求書の見本が記載されています。

paragraphs.text:
If the insurer decides to arrange a medical examination (at its own expense), it enables the insurer to be satisfied that:
▪ the alleged accident has caused the injuries; and
▪ such injuries are preventing the insured person from working; or
▪ the injury is as described and is causing the disability sustained.
paragraphs.textJP:
保険者が（自己の費用負担で）医療検査を手配する場合、保険者は以下を確認することができます：
▪ 主張された事故が傷害を引き起こしたこと
▪ かかる傷害が被保険者の就労を妨げていること
▪ 傷害が記述のとおりであり、被った障害を引き起こしていること

paragraphs.text: Sometimes, the insurer has the option of requesting a post-mortem, which is useful when the insurer suspects that death has resulted from natural causes.
paragraphs.textJP: 場合によっては、保険者は死後検案を依頼する権限を有します。これは、保険者が死亡が自然の原因によるものであると疑う場合に有用です。

paragraphs.text: The various requirements of this condition are conditions precedent to liability, meaning compliance with them is required, if there is to be a valid claim.
paragraphs.textJP: この条件の各要件は賠償責任の前提条件であり、有効な請求が成立するためにはこれらの遵守が必要です。

paragraphs.text: Any change in the occupation of the insured has a bearing on the risk, particularly as the premium is determined mainly according to his occupation. Hence, there will usually be a condition stating that the insured will give the insurer written notice of any change of occupation. This enables the insurer to re-underwrite, if appropriate, to consider continuation of the insurance, the rate of premium, and any conditions that may be applied.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者の職業の変更はリスクに影響を及ぼします。特に保険料は主に被保険者の職業に基づいて決定されるためです。したがって、被保険者が職業の変更について保険者に書面で通知する旨の条件が通常設けられています。これにより、保険者は必要に応じて再引受を行い、保険の継続、保険料率、適用される条件を検討することができます。

paragraphs.text: Also, it may be common for insurers to further specify that any changes in “habits, pursuits or of any disease, injury or physical defect or infirmity” be reported to the insurer, as such factors may influence the risk insured, thus requiring a review of the premium payable by the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: また、保険者は「習慣、趣味、または疾病、傷害その他の身体的障害もしくは虚弱」の変更についても保険者に報告することを求めるのが一般的です。かかる要因は付保されたリスクに影響する可能性があり、被保険者が支払う保険料の見直しが必要になるためです。

paragraphs.text: This is similar to the cancellation condition common under almost all insurance contracts. The cancellation condition enables the insurer to avoid protracted negotiation in event that the insurer seeks to terminate a risk that becomes abnormal during the currency of the policy. This may be advisable for example, because of an unfavourable moral hazard uncovered at the time of settlement of a claim, such as an insured who suffers what insurers believe is a staged “accidental injury”, as the insured was heavily in debt and required cash.
paragraphs.textJP: これは、ほぼすべての保険契約に共通する解約条件と類似しています。解約条件により、保険者は保険期間中にリスクが異常となった場合に、長引く交渉を避けてリスクの終了を追求できます。これは例えば、保険金精算時に明らかになった好ましくないモラルハザード（例：多額の借金を抱え現金を必要としていた被保険者が「偶発的傷害」を仕組んだと保険者が考える場合）のために望ましい場合があります。

paragraphs.text: This condition protects the insurer’s interest and makes the position clear to the insured. The insured must dutifully fulfil the conditions before the insurer can be liable for any claim under the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: この条件は保険者の利益を保護し、被保険者に対して立場を明確にするものです。被保険者は保険証券に基づく請求について保険者が責任を負うためには、条件を忠実に遵守しなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: Proposal forms are often used in the application process for PA Insurance (see Appendix 3D for a sample proposal form for PA Insurance).
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険の申込みには申込書が使用されることが多いです（傷害保険の申込書見本は付録3D参照）。

paragraphs.text: Premium rates are the same for men and women, with age being an important consideration. However, the primary underwriting factor for this class of business is the profession or occupation of the insured. Another important factor that affects rating is the level of cover or benefits chosen by the insured, since a PA Insurance policy offers a wide range of benefits that the insured may select. Finally, health and physical impairments may also influence the pricing, since such factors also relate, to a certain extent, to the insured’s susceptibility to accidents.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険料率は男女同一であり、年齢が重要な考慮事項となります。ただし、この保険種目の保険料率を決定する主な引受上の要因は、被保険者の専門職業または職業です。料率設定に影響するもう一つの重要な要因は、被保険者が選択する補償レベルまたは給付内容です。傷害保険は被保険者が選択できる幅広い給付を提供するためです。最後に、健康状態や身体的障害も価格設定に影響する場合があります。かかる要因は、被保険者の事故への感受性にもある程度関連するためです。

paragraphs.text: Individuals who purchase PA Insurance represent many types of occupations. The pricing of each risk reflects the varying degrees of hazards in the occupations. White-collar workers such as professionals and office administrators represent lower and better risks, from the perspective of frequency and severity of work accidents, when compared to blue-collar workers (e.g. assembly-line workers, construction workers). Therefore, the premium for PA Insurance reflects the occupation of the insured at the time of policy purchase or renewal.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険を購入する個人はさまざまな職業に就いています。各リスクの料率設定は、職業における危険度の程度を反映しています。事務職の専門家やオフィス管理者などのホワイトカラー労働者は、ブルーカラー労働者（例：組立ライン作業員、建設作業員）と比較して、労働災害の頻度と重大性の観点から、より低くより良好なリスクを示します。したがって、傷害保険の保険料は、保険証券の購入時または更新時における被保険者の職業を反映します。

paragraphs.text:
Occupation provides a fair basis for premium rating, but it is not necessary to fix a rate for every single occupation. It is sufficient to group similar occupations into broad classes for rating purposes. Although some insurers may have more classes of classification, many insurers commonly use three classes for grouping occupations, such as the examples as described below:
▪ Class I: Persons engaged in professional, managerial, administrative, clerical and non-manual work solely in the office or similar non-hazardous places, such as accountants, administrators, dentists, doctors, lawyers, etc.
▪ Class II: Persons engaged in work of a supervisory nature not involved in the use of machinery or tools, and not exposed to any special hazards, such as chauffeurs, salesmen (outdoor), surveyors, waiters, tour guides, etc.
▪ Class III: Persons engaged in manual work not of particular hazardous nature, but involved in the use of machinery or tools, such as contractors, electricians, hawkers, plumbers, mechanics, etc.
paragraphs.textJP:
職業は保険料率設定の公正な基準を提供しますが、あらゆる単一の職業に対して料率を設定する必要はありません。類似した職業を料率設定の目的で大まかなクラスに分類すれば十分です。保険者によってはより多くの分類クラスを持つ場合がありますが、多くの保険者は通常、以下の例のとおり3つのクラスを使用して職業を分類しています：
▪ クラスI：専門職、管理職、事務職および非肉体労働に従事し、オフィスまたは類似の非危険な場所でのみ勤務する者（例：会計士、管理者、歯科医、医師、弁護士等）
▪ クラスII：監督的な性質の業務に従事し、機械や工具の使用に関与せず、特別な危険にさらされていない者（例：運転手、営業（屋外）、測量士、ウェイター、ツアーガイド等）
▪ クラスIII：特に危険な性質ではない肉体労働に従事するが、機械や工具の使用に関与する者（例：請負業者、電気技師、行商人、配管工、整備士等）

paragraphs.text: For each classification, the insurer uses a basic (manual) rate for a stated unit of coverage, and the premium calculated according to the number of units required to provide an appropriate coverage. The following Table 3.2 provides an example of this: Table 3.2: Example Of Premiums Benefits For Each Amount Of S$ Annual Premium (S$) Class Of Occupation I II III Death & Permanent Disablement 10,000 6.00 7.00 9.00 Temporary Total/Partial Disablement 100/50 Per Week
paragraphs.textJP:
各分類について、保険者は所定の補償単位に対する基本（マニュアル）料率を使用し、適切な補償を提供するために必要な単位数に応じて保険料を計算します。以下の表3.2はその例を示しています：
表3.2：保険料の例
給付 / 各S$あたり / 年間保険料（S$）
職業分類 I / II / III
死亡・永久障害 10,000 / 6.00 / 7.00 / 9.00
一時的完全／部分就業不能 100/50（週あたり）

paragraphs.text: The above classification of occupations excludes occupations of a generally highly hazardous nature, as well as pricing for supplementary benefits. In rating insureds engaged in hazardous occupations, insurers will usually impose extra loadings to the standard premium rates. Insurers will usually charge additional premiums for the additional benefits selected by the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 上記の職業分類は、一般に危険度が極めて高い職業を除外しています。また、追加給付に対する料率設定も除外しています。危険な職業に従事する被保険者の料率設定においては、保険者は通常、標準保険料率に追加の割増を課します。被保険者が選択した追加給付に対しては、保険者は通常、追加保険料を徴収します。

paragraphs.text: Insurers will often avoid coverage for specifically hazardous professions, such as oil riggers; timber loggers; air or sea crew, military, naval or police force members; horse jockeys; and professional sport personnel. Some insurers will exclude them entirely, while others will consider additional premiums or special loadings to cover some of them, depending on the business considerations.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、石油掘削作業員、木材伐採作業員、空中・海上の乗務員、軍隊・海軍・警察の構成員、騎手、プロのスポーツ選手等の特に危険な職業については、補償を避けることが多いです。一部の保険者はこれらを完全に除外しますが、他の保険者は事業上の考慮に応じて、追加保険料または特別な割増を条件にそのうちの一部について引受を検討します。

paragraphs.text: A key point in pricing PA Insurance is to ascertain the level of benefits that the insured selects to buy. An insured may have a policy for accidental death protection, as well as permanent total disablement of major limbs and loss of sight. He may also carry a policy with coverage for other Permanent Disablement, Temporary Disablement, Medical Expenses, and Hospital Cash benefits. Hence, the insurer will commonly examine the types of benefits that an insured wants, before charging the premiums for them.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険の料率設定において重要なのは、被保険者が購入する給付の水準を確認することです。被保険者は、偶発的死亡保護および主要四肢の永久全身障害・視力喪失に対する保険証券を持つ場合があります。また、その他の永久障害、一時的障害、医療費および入院日額給付金の補償を含む保険証券を持つこともあります。したがって、保険者は被保険者が希望する給付の種類を確認してから保険料を算出するのが通常です。

paragraphs.text: However, as insurers normally offer PA Insurance as a “package” plan, the intending insured can choose the type of plan with the appropriate levels of benefits and limits to suit his needs.
paragraphs.textJP: ただし、保険者は通常、傷害保険を「パッケージ」プランとして提供しているため、加入希望者は自身のニーズに合った適切な給付水準と限度額を持つプランの種類を選択できます。

paragraphs.text: PA underwriters should pay special attention to those accidents or illnesses that appear to be repetitive, as these previous events and impairments are potential causes of subsequent recurring claims.
paragraphs.textJP: 傷害保険の引受担当者は、反復的に見える事故や疾病に特に注意を払う必要があります。これらの過去の発生や障害は、その後の反復的な請求の潜在的な原因です。

paragraphs.text: Current health conditions may also require careful examination. Although the presence of impairment may not necessarily mean an increased risk, instead of declining cover, it may necessitate other underwriting actions by varying the policy conditions. For example, if the proposer has lost the sight of one eye, the underwriter may adjust the benefits payable for total loss of sight and issue an endorsement accordingly. The underwriter may also exclude coverage for all claims arising from certain medical conditions that are continuous, and that can be repetitive in the future.
paragraphs.textJP: 現在の健康状態も慎重な検討が必要な場合があります。障害の存在は必ずしもリスクの増加を意味するわけではありませんが、引き受けを拒否するのではなく、保険証券の条件を変更するなど、他の引受上の措置が必要になる場合があります。例えば、申込者が片眼の視力を失っている場合、引受担当者は完全な視力喪失に対して支払われる給付金を調整し、それに応じた裏書を発行する場合があります。また、引受担当者は、継続的であり将来反復する可能性のある特定の医療上の状態から生じるすべての請求の補償を除外する場合もあります。

paragraphs.text:
Usually, the policy will specify that its coverage terminates, upon the occurrence of:
▪ death of the insured; or
▪ an injury which causes permanent total disability, such that the compensation under the policy equals to 100% of the capital sum insured (or, for some insurers, 150% of the capital sum insured).
paragraphs.textJP:
通常、保険証券は以下のいずれかの事由の発生により補償が終了する旨を規定しています：
▪ 被保険者の死亡
▪ 保険証券に基づく補償が保険金額の100%（一部の保険者では150%）に相当する永久全身障害を引き起こす傷害

paragraphs.text: Payment by the insurer in full settlement of the insured’s death or injury claim as described above shall represent a final and complete discharge of the insurer’s liability under the policy, and the policy shall thereafter cease to provide insurance for the insured.
paragraphs.textJP: 上記の死亡または傷害請求に対する保険者の全額支払いは、保険証券に基づく保険者の責任の最終的かつ完全な免除を表し、保険証券はその後被保険者に対する保険の提供を停止します。

paragraphs.text: Claim documents include the claim form (see sample in Appendix 3C) and other supporting documents, depending on the types of benefits claimed.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険金請求書類には、保険金請求書（見本は付録3C参照）および請求される給付の種類に応じた証拠書類が含まれます。

これらの裏付け書類には以下が含まれます：
▪ 事故の警察報告書（悪意による行為が関与している場合）
▪ 死亡証明書（死亡給付の請求の場合）
▪ 医療報告書（障害を証明するため）
▪ 診断書、入院退院サマリー、および医療費の原本の請求書、送り状、領収書（医療費の実費てん補および／または入院日額給付金の請求のため）

paragraphs.text: It is usually a policy condition that all supporting documents for claims are at the insured’s expense. If an insurer wishes to clarify or confirm a medical condition, the insurer may require the insured to undergo a medical examination, usually with one of its appointed panel of doctors and at the insurer’s expense. Appendix 3A ABC INSURANCE COMPANY (SINGAPORE) LIMITED (Registration No: 2009-12345-A) 21 Any Street, ABC Centre, Singapore 654321 Tel: (65) 6789 8181 Fax: (65) 6789 8282 E-mail: contactus@abcinco.com.sg Website: www.abcinco.com.sg PERSONAL ACCIDENT INSURANCE POLICY DEFINITION OF WORDS Certain words have been defined below. These have the same meaning wherever they are used in the Policy or the Schedule. They appear in bold print (e.g. Insured Person, Injury) or begin with a capital letter (e.g. You, We). We, Us, Our, the Company ABC Insurance Company (Singapore) Limited You, Your, the Insured The policy owner named as Insured in the Schedule. Insured Person Each of the persons named as such in the Schedule who meets the eligibility criteria set out in General Condition 3 of the Policy. Schedule The Schedule containing details of the Insured Person(s), type of cover selected and Period of Insurance. The Schedule forms part of the Policy. Period of Insurance The period shown in the Schedule, and any further period for which You agree to pay and We agree to accept premium. Commencement Date Original inception date of cover under this Policy as shown in the Schedule. Child A person named as the Insured Person in the Schedule who is the Insured's biological or legally adopted and unmarried Child aged between one year to 18 years or 23 years if pursuing full-time education in a recognised tertiary institution within his/her Usual Country of Residence as Singapore. Accident An event which happens suddenly and gives rise to a result which the Insured Person did not intend or anticipate. Injury Bodily injury suffered anywhere in the world caused solely by Accident and not by sickness, disease or gradual physical or mental wear and tear. Physician A qualified medical practitioner other than You, Your relative, the Insured Person or the Insured Person's relative who is licensed by the Medical Authorities of the country in which treatment is provided to practise Western medicine and surgery, and who in rendering such treatment is practicing within the scope of his or her licensing and training in his/her geographical area of practice. Hospital A lawfully operating institution for the care and treatment of sick and injured persons, which has 24 hours nursing services by registered graduate nurses, one or more Physicians available at all times and organised facilities for diagnosis and major surgery, which shall not primarily be a clinic, a place for alcoholics or drug addicts, a nursing, rest or convalescent home, home for the aged or similar establishment. Chinese Physician A person other than You, Your relative, the Insured Person or the Insured Person's relative engaging in the practice of traditional Chinese medicine and/or acupuncture (including a herbalist, bonesetter or chiropractor), who is duly licensed or registered to practise according to the laws and regulations applicable in the geographical area of his/her practice. Daily Hospitalisation Cash Benefit Cash benefit payable at an agreed sum per day in the event of an Insured Person's confinement in a Hospital for a continuous uninterrupted period of at least 24 hours upon the advice of a Physician for which the Hospital makes a charge for room and board. Usual Country of Residence The country in which the Insured Person is usually living at the inception of this insurance. As a condition precedent to liability, the Company must be informed in writing of any change in the Usual Country of Residence. A permanent change in the Usual Country of Residence is deemed to occur when the Insured Person lives or intends to live in another country for more than 6 consecutive months during any Period of Insurance. The Company reserves the right to continue cover on the terms and conditions it considers appropriate to the new country of residence or to decline to continue cover under the Policy. Burns 3rd degree burns. Weekly Benefit Compensation payable at a rate per week. THE BENEFITS We will pay You: 1. The Compensation for death or disablement (the Results) as described below if the Insured Person suffers Injury which within 104 weeks of its happening is the sale cause of the death or such disablement. 2. Weekly Benefit shall not be payable for: (a) any period of time subsequent to the death of the Insured Person or subsequent to Compensation becoming payable under any part of Result B or C. (b) both Results 01 and 02 for the same period of disablement. 3. Weekly Benefit for either Results D1 or 02 shall be payable when the total amount has been agreed, or at Your request at intervals of not less than four weeks (but not in advance) commencing four weeks after receipt by Us of written notice of the Injury. 4. The maximum period the Company will pay under Result F for anyone Injury is up to 50 consecutive days subject to the Overall Limit indicated in the Schedule for anyone Period of Insurance. 5. Nothing will be payable in respect of Result E if there is any other insurance in force covering the loss or if You or the Insured Person are entitled to indemnity from any other source, provided that We shall not be relieved of liability under this Result so far as concerns any excess beyond the amount payable under such other insurance or indemnity. 6. The limits of compensation specified above will apply regardless of the number of times the Policy is renewed. OVERALL COMPENSATION LIMIT Our maximum aggregate liability in respect of all Insured Persons travelling in one aircraft or surface transport vehicle or vessel shall not exceed the Conveyance Limit of SGD5,000,000 or the aggregate of the amount of Compensation payable in respect of such Insured Persons, whichever is the lesser. If the aggregate amount of all claims under this Policy in respect of all Insured Persons travelling in one conveyance exceeds the Conveyance Limit, the Company's liability in respect of each of such Insured Persons will be a rateable proportion of the benefits due in respect of that person. EXTRA BENEFITS
paragraphs.textJP:
通常、保険金請求の証拠書類の費用は被保険者が負担する旨が約款条件となっています。保険者が医療上の状態を確認したい場合、保険者が指定する医師による医療検査を被保険者の費用負担で受けることを求めます。

付録3A：傷害保険 保険金請求書（サンプル）
ABC INSURANCE COMPANY (SINGAPORE) LIMITED
パートA：被保険者詳細（氏名・NRIC・住所・連絡先・職業・生年月日・保険証券番号）
パートB：事故詳細（事故日時・場所・原因・傷害の内容・警察報告番号）
パートC：医療詳細（医療機関名・治療日・診断結果・入院期間・就業不能期間）
パートD：請求する給付（死亡 / 永久障害 / 一時的障害 / 医療費 / 入院日額）
パートE：告知および承認（真実性の宣言・医療記録開示承認・署名）
主治医報告書（患者氏名・診断・傷害の性質・治療内容・入院期間・障害の程度・既往歴・医師署名）

paragraphs.text: (a) Where an Insured and his/her legal spouse are insured at the same time under this Policy, up to 3 of the Insured's biological or legally adopted, unmarried Children (aged between one year to 18 years or 23 years if pursuing full-time education in a recognised tertiary institution within their Usual Country of Residence as Singapore) shall be covered at 10% of the Sum Insured of the Insured or his/her insured spouse (whichever is the higher Sum Insured) for the following Results for each Child named in the Schedule: Result A. Death Result B. Permanent and Total Disablement Result E. Medical Expenses (b) Where the Insured is insured on his/her own under this Policy and subject to the payment of the additional required premium, up to 3 of the Insured's biological or legally adopted, unmarried Children (aged between one year to 18 years or 23 years if pursuing full time education in a recognised tertiary institution within their Usual Country of Residence as Singapore) shall be covered at 10% of the Sum Insured of the Insured for the following Results for each Child named in the Schedule: Result A. Death Result B. Permanent and Total Disablement Result E. Medical Expenses
paragraphs.textJP:
(a) 被保険者とその法定配偶者が同時に本保険証券に基づき付保されている場合、被保険者の実子または法的養子で未婚の子供（1歳から18歳まで、または所在国の認定された高等教育機関で全日制教育を受けている場合は23歳まで）最大3名が、被保険者またはその被保険配偶者の保険金額（いずれか高い方）の10%で、附則に記載された各子供について以下の結果を補償されます：
結果A：死亡
結果B：永久完全障害
結果E：医療費
(b) 被保険者が本保険証券で単独で付保されている場合、追加保険料の支払いを条件として、被保険者の実子または法的養子で未婚の子供（1歳から18歳まで、または所在国の認定された高等教育機関で全日制教育を受けている場合は23歳まで）最大3名が、被保険者の保険金額の10%で、附則に記載された各子供について以下の結果を補償されます：
結果A：死亡
結果B：永久完全障害
結果E：医療費

sections.title: Loss And Damage To Clothing And Personal Belongings
sections.titleJP: 衣類および携行品の滅失・損害

paragraphs.text: The Company undertakes to pay to the Insured Person who sustains Injury and damage to clothing and personal belongings worn or carried by such Insured Person in the same Accident (but only to the extent that they are not recoverable from any other source), an amount not exceeding SGD500 for such loss or damage arising out of anyone Injury provided that the Medical Expense benefit under Result F is also payable under this Policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険会社は、被保険者が傷害を被り、同一の事故においてその傷害の直接の結果として当該被保険者が着用または携行していた衣類および携行品に損害が生じた場合（他の供給源から回収可能でない範囲に限り）、かかる滅失・損害に対して1事故あたりS$500を限度として支払います。

paragraphs.text: Upon each renewal of this Policy, the original Sum Insured in respect of each Insured Person for Result A (Death) and Result B (Permanent and Total Disablement) at the Commencement Date will be increased by 10% as Renewal Bonus for the renewed Period of Insurance provided that: (i) no claim has arisen in respect of the Insured Person during the preceding Period of Insurance in respect of: a) Result A (Death); b) Result B (Permanent and Total Disablement); c) Result C (Permanent Partial Disablement) d) Result D1 (Temporary Total Disablement) e) Result D2 (Temporary Partial Disablement); f) Result F (Hospitalisation Cash Benefits) g) Result G (Recuperation Benefit) h) Result H (Bereavement Grant); (ii) the increase in the original Sum Insured as Renewal Bonus shall be for the first three consecutive 12- month renewals of the Policy only; (iii) the Renewal Bonus does not apply to any Child Insured Person; and (iv) the Renewal Bonus does not apply to the Terrorism extension under Extra Benefit 4 below.
paragraphs.textJP:
本保険証券の更新ごとに、各被保険者の死亡（結果A）および永久完全障害（結果B）に関する開始日時点の当初の保険金額は、以下の条件を満たす場合、更新後の保険期間の更新ボーナスとして10%増額されます：
(i) 直前の保険期間中に当該被保険者について以下の結果に関する請求が発生していないこと：
a) 結果A（死亡）
b) 結果B（永久完全障害）
c) 結果C（永久部分障害）
d) 結果D1（一時的完全障害）
e) 結果D2（一時的部分障害）
f) 結果F（入院日額給付金）
g) 結果G（療養給付金）
h) 結果H（弔慰金）
(ii) 更新ボーナスとしての当初保険金額の増額は、保険証券の最初の連続3回の12ヶ月更新にのみ適用される
(iii) 更新ボーナスは子供の被保険者には適用されない
(iv) 更新ボーナスは下記の追加給付4のテロリズム拡張補償には適用されない

paragraphs.text: Where the Insured requests for an increase or reduction to the Sum Insured for an Insured Person in respect of Result A (Death) and Result B (Permanent and Total Disablement) during any Period of Insurance and agreed to by Us, We will increase the revised Sum Insured for that Insured Person by 10% as Renewal Bonus upon the renewal of the Policy for the renewed Period of Insurance provided that (i) the request to revise the Sum Insured is made by the Insured within the first six months of the relevant Period of Insurance; (ii) any accumulated Renewal Bonus for the Period(s) of Insurance prior to the Period of Insurance in which the Sum Insured was revised, will lapse and not be added on; (iii) no claim has arisen in respect of the Insured Person during the preceding Period of Insurance in respect of any or all of the Results listed in 3.1.(i) above; (iv) the increase in the revised Sum Insured as Renewal Bonus shall only be for the first three consecutive 12- month renewals of the Policy following the Period of Insurance in which the Sum Insured was revised; (v) the Renewal Bonus does not apply to any Child Insured Person; and (vi) the Renewal Bonus does not apply to the Terrorism extension under Extra Benefit 4 below.
paragraphs.textJP:
被保険者がいずれかの保険期間中に、被保険者の死亡（結果A）および永久完全障害（結果B）に関する保険金額の増額または減額を要請し、当社が同意した場合、以下の条件を満たすことを前提として、保険証券の更新時に当該被保険者の改定後保険金額を更新ボーナスとして10%増額します：
(i) 保険金額の改定要請が、該当する保険期間の最初の6ヶ月以内に被保険者により行われること
(ii) 保険金額が改定された保険期間より前の保険期間に累積した更新ボーナスは失効し、加算されない
(iii) 直前の保険期間中に当該被保険者について、上記3.1.(i)に記載されたいずれかまたはすべての結果に関する請求が発生していないこと
(iv) 更新ボーナスとしての改定後保険金額の増額は、保険金額が改定された保険期間後の最初の連続3回の12ヶ月更新にのみ適用される
(v) 更新ボーナスは子供の被保険者には適用されない
(vi) 更新ボーナスは下記の追加給付4のテロリズム拡張補償には適用されない

paragraphs.text: No additional premium is required for the Renewal Bonus.
paragraphs.textJP: 更新ボーナスに追加保険料は不要です。

paragraphs.text: This Policy is extended to cover the Insured Person against death or disablement (the following Results) as described below if the Insured Person suffers an Injury which within 104 weeks of its happening is the sole cause of the death or such disablement and the Injury is sustained through acts of terrorism provided that there is no liability when such acts of terrorism involve the use of biological, chemical agents or nuclear devices: Result A Death Result B Permanent and Total Disablement Result C Permanent Partial Disablement Result D1 Temporary Total Disablement Result D2 Temporary Partial Disablement Result E Medical Expenses Result F Daily Hospitalisation Cash Benefits Result G Recuperation Benefit Result H Bereavement Grant The limit for each of the Result above is per the specified Sum Insured applicable to the Result. The aggregate limit for all the above Results is up to the Sum Insured for Result A (Death benefit) at the Commencement Date of this Policy or in the event of an increase or reduction to the Sum Insured for Result A (Death benefit) and Result B (permanent and Total Disablement) as requested by the Insured and agreed to by Us during any Period of Insurance, up to the applicable increased or decreased Sum Insured for Result A (Death benefit) or SGD250,000 for each Insured Person, whichever is lesser. Cover under this extension for each Child Insured Person is in respect of Result A, Result B and Result E only. The limit of this cover is at 10% of the Sum Insured under Result A, Result B and Result E of the Insured. Where the Insured and his/her legal spouse are insured at the same time under this Policy, the limit shall be that of the Sum Insured of the Insured or his/her insured spouse, whichever is the higher Sum Insured for Result A, Result B and Result E.
paragraphs.textJP:
本保険証券は、被保険者が傷害を被り、その発生から104週間以内にその傷害が唯一の原因として以下に記載する死亡または障害（結果）を引き起こした場合で、当該傷害がテロ行為を通じて被った場合に、被保険者を補償するよう拡張されます。ただし、テロ行為が生物・化学兵器または核装置の使用を伴う場合は責任を負いません：
結果A：死亡
結果B：永久完全障害
結果C：永久部分障害
結果D1：一時的完全障害
結果D2：一時的部分障害
結果E：医療費
結果F：入院日額給付金
結果G：療養給付金
結果H：弔慰金
上記の各結果の限度額は、当該結果に適用される規定の保険金額に基づきます。上記すべての結果の合算限度額は、本保険証券の開始日時点の結果A（死亡給付）の保険金額、またはいずれかの保険期間中に被保険者が要請し当社が同意した結果A（死亡給付）および結果B（永久完全障害）の保険金額の増額もしくは減額が行われた場合は該当する増額もしくは減額後の結果A（死亡給付）の保険金額、もしくは各被保険者あたりS50,000のいずれか低い方までとします。
子供の被保険者に対する本拡張補償は、結果A、結果B、結果Eについてのみ適用されます。この補償の限度額は、被保険者の結果A、結果B、結果Eの保険金額の10%です。被保険者とその法定配偶者が同時に本保険証券に基づき付保されている場合、限度額は被保険者またはその被保険配偶者の結果A、結果B、結果Eの保険金額のいずれか高い方とします。

paragraphs.text: This Policy is extended to cover Injury sustained by the Insured Person while on part-time National Service as a Reservist in the Navy, Army, Air Force, Police, Fire Brigade or Vigilante Corps, provided that We will not be liable to Pay any compensation if the Insured Person was taking part in or was present at any military, navy or air force Operation during actual warfare or any insurrection or any expedition or operation of a warlike character either as a Combatant or non-combatant when the Injury was sustained.
paragraphs.textJP: 本保険証券は、被保険者が海軍、陸軍、空軍、警察、消防署または自警団のパートタイムの国民兵役（予備役）に従事している間に被った傷害を補償するよう拡張されます。ただし、被保険者が射撃場の外での銃の使用を含む訓練もしくは作戦に参加していた場合、または被保険者の過失により生じた傷害の場合、当社は補償金を支払う責任を負いません。

paragraphs.text: This Policy is extended to cover the Insured Person against death caused by Injury or Injury as a result of Riot & Strike. The Company shall not be liable for any claim arising out or in connection with the Insured Person's own participation or provocation of any such act or if such act could reasonably have been avoided by the Insured Person.
paragraphs.textJP: 本保険証券は、暴動およびストライキの結果として傷害により生じた被保険者の死亡または傷害を補償するよう拡張されます。保険会社は、被保険者自身の参加もしくは挑発によるかかる行為から生じた、またはかかる行為が被保険者の行動の合理的な結果として予見できた場合の請求については責任を負いません。

paragraphs.text: This Policy is extended to cover motor-cycling (whether as a rider or pillion-rider) provided that at the time of Accident, the Insured Person is wearing a safety helmet, and not engaging in or practising for racing and hill climbing contests, reliability trials, speed or duration testing.
paragraphs.textJP: 本保険証券は、事故発生時に被保険者が安全ヘルメットを着用し、レースやヒルクライム競技、信頼性試走、スピードまたは耐久テストのための練習や参加をしていないことを条件として、二輪車の運転（運転者または同乗者として）を補償するよう拡張されます。

paragraphs.text: This Policy is extended to cover the Insured Person against death caused by Injury or Injury as a result of Burns subject to the limit as set out under item 18 of Result C. The company shall not be liable for any claim for such death or Injury arising out of or in connection with the Insured Person's own wilful or intentional act.
paragraphs.textJP: 本保険証券は、結果Cの項目18に規定された限度額に従い、火傷の結果として傷害により生じた被保険者の死亡または傷害を補償するよう拡張されます。保険会社は、被保険者自身の故意の行為から生じた、またはこれに関連するかかる死亡または傷害の請求については責任を負いません。

sections.title: Suffocation By Smoke, Poisonous Fumes, Gas And Drowning
sections.titleJP: 煙・有毒ガス・ガスによる窒息および溺死

paragraphs.text: Any Result sustained by an Insured Person due to suffocation by smoke, poisonous fumes, gas and drowning shall be deemed to be Injury sustained by an Insured Person provided that such Injury does not arise out of an Insured Person's wilful and intentional act.
paragraphs.textJP: 煙、有毒ガス、ガスによる窒息および溺死の結果として被保険者が被ったいかなる結果も、被保険者が被った傷害とみなされます。ただし、かかる傷害が被保険者の故意かつ意図的な行為から生じたものでないことを条件とします。

paragraphs.text: We shall presume death to have been suffered by the Insured Person if he or she is missing for twelve consecutive months, and sufficient evidence is provided that leads Us to the conclusion that death was caused by an Injury. However, if at any time after payment of Compensation under this Policy for such death the Insured Person is found to be living, such Compensation shall be refunded to Us.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者が12か月連続で行方不明であり、傷害が死因であるとの結論に至るのに十分な証拠が提供された場合、当社は死亡したものと推定します。ただし、本保険証券に基づく補償金の支払い後のいかなる時点でも被保険者の生存が判明した場合、支払われた補償金は返還されなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: If an Insured Person suffers an Injury and then, in consequence of that Injury suffers death or disablement as a result of exposure to the elements, We will consider such death or disablement as having been caused by an Injury.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者が傷害を被り、その後当該傷害の結果として自然の要素（悪天候や環境条件）への暴露により死亡または障害が生じた場合、当社はかかる死亡または障害を傷害に起因するものとみなします。

paragraphs.text:
This Policy is extended to cover any Injury happening to the Insured Person while engaged in sports as a form of recreation and not on a professional basis except for: (a) parachuting (b) hang gliding (c) any kind of race (other than on foot or swimming) or trial of speed or reliability (d) potholing, mountaineering or rock climbing necessitating the use of guides or ropes (e) underwater activities necessitating the use of compressed air or gas All Extra Benefits are subject to the Terms, Conditions, and Exceptions of this Policy. GENERAL CONDITIONS The conditions which appear in the Policy or in any Endorsement are part of the contract and must be complied with. They are where their nature permit conditions precedent to the right to recover from Us. 1. Co-operation As a condition precedent to the Company's liability, the Insured Person or his/her representatives shall co-operate fully with the Company and its medical advisers and will fully and faithfully disclose all material facts and matters which the Insured Person knows or ought to know and will upon request as the Company may reasonably make execute any document to empower the Company to obtain relevant information, at the Insured or the Insured Person's expense, from any Physician, Hospital or other source. 2. Reasonable Precautions and Material Changes The Insured Person shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent and minimise any Injury. The Company must be informed immediatelyi n writing as soon as You are aware of any change in the employment, occupation, duties or pursuits of any Insured Person, or any other change which may increase the possibility of a claim under this Policy. The Company reserves the right to continue cover on terms and conditions it considers appropriate to such changes in material information or circumstances or to decline to continue cover under the Policy. 3. Eligibility Unless We agree in writing otherwise, any person You wish to insure under this Policy must be named as an Insured Person in the Schedule and must at the Commencement Date of the Policy be the following: (a) Yourself aged between 18 years and 65 years old, or (b) Your legal spouse aged between 18 years and 65 years old, or (c) Your biological or legally adopted, unmarried Child aged between 1 year to 18 years or 23 years if pursuing full-time education in a recognised tertiary institution within his/her Usual Country of Residence as Singapore. 4. Alterations (a) We have the right to vary the premium payable and all other terms, conditions and exceptions of the Policy by giving the Insured 30 days' notice of such variation(s). (b) If the date of birth of the Insured Person has been incorrectly stated, the benefits will be amended by Us having regard to the true date of birth. If the true date of birth is such that, had it been known to Us at the time of the Policy was proposed for, We would not have issued the Policy, then We may cancel the Policy and no benefits will be payable. (c) Any other misrepresentation of or failure to disclose material facts by the Insured or Insured Person, will entitle the Company to alter, amend or cancel the Policy having regard to the true facts. A material fact is any information that could influence the Company in its assessment of the proposal. 5. Cancellation Either the Insured or the Company may cancel this Policy by giving the other party 14 days notice in writing sent to the last known address. Refunds of premium in respect of a Period of Insurance will be made as follows: (a) If the Insured cancels the Policy, the Company will refund the Insured the premium paid less the premium calculated at Our Short Period rates from the date of cancellation provided no claim has arisen in relation to that Period of Insurance and the amount refundable is more than SGD10.00. (b) If the Company cancels the Policy, the Company will make a pro-rata refund of the premium paid. 6. Termination (a) The entire Policy will terminate and all Insured Persons' cover under it will cease immediately upon: i) non-payment of premium by the due date as described in the Payment Before Cover Warranty of this Policy; or ii) the cancellation of this Policy as described in General Condition 5. (b) Unless We have agreed otherwise in writing, the cover of an Insured Person under this Policy will terminate immediately in any of the following circumstances, whichever first occurs: i) when the Insured Person's Usual Country of Residence ceases to be Singapore ii) where an Insured Person on the expiry of the Period of Insurance during which he or she attains the age of 76 years iii) where the Insured Person is a Child of the Insured, on the expiry of the Period of Insurance during which he/she attains the age of 19 years, or 24 years if studying full-time at a recognised tertiary institution within his/her Usual Country of Residence as Singapore. 7. In the Event of Fraud If any claim shall in any respect be false or fraudulent or if fraudulent means or devices are used by the Insured Person(s) or anyone acting on their behalf to obtain benefit under this Policy, then the Policy will be cancelled immediately and all benefit and premium forfeited. 8. No Trust The Company will not recognise or be affected by any notice of trust, charge or assignment relating to this Policy and the Insured's receipt or that of the Insured's legal personal representative shall in all cases effectively discharge Our liability. 9. Legal Personal Representatives The terms, exceptions and conditions of this Policy, so far as applicable and with necessary modifications, shall apply to the legal personal representatives of the Insured or the Insured Person. 10. Exclusion of Rights Under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act A person who is not a party to this Policy shall have no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act to enforce any of its terms. Insured Persons (other than the Insured) are not parties to this Policy contract. CLAIMS CONDITIONS The payment of claims under this Policy is dependent upon observance of its terms and conditions by You, and so far as they apply, by the Insured Person or any other claimant. 1. You must report in writing to us as soon as reasonably possible, full details of any Injury which may result in a claim under this Policy. 2. You or the Insured Person shall employ the services of a registered medical practitioner and the Insured Person shall undergo any treatment such practitioner shall deem necessary. 3. All certificates, information and evidence must be provided at your expense or at the expense of any claimant in the form and nature required. 4. The Insured Person may have to undergo further medical examination required by Us at Our expense. 5. In the event of death of the Insured Person, We shall require sight of the death certificate and may require a post-mortem examination at Our expense. 6. Where we have accepted a claim but the amount to be paid is in dispute, the matter shall be referred to an independent arbitrator acceptable to the parties involved. Where any dispute is by this Condition to be referred to arbitration, the making of an award shall be a condition precedent to any right of action against the Company. 7. If the Company offers an amount in settlement or disclaims liability altogether for a claim, and such a claim is not within twelve calendar months from the date of such an offer or disclaimer referred to arbitration as required under Condition 6 or made the subject of a pending court action, the claim shall be deemed to be abandoned and the Company shall have no liability in respect of it. GENERAL EXCEPTIONS We will not pay any sum under this Policy for: 1. Injury directly or indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any of the following regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any other sequence to the Injury: (a) ionising radiations from or contamination by radioactivity from any nuclear fuel or from any nuclear waste or from the combustion of nuclear fuel (b) the radioactive, toxic, explosive or other hazardous or contaminating properties of any nuclear installation, reactor or other nuclear assembly or nuclear components thereof (c) any weapon or device employing atomic or nuclear fission and/or fusion or other like reaction or radioactive force or matter (d) the radioactive, toxic, explosive or other hazardous or contaminating properties of any radioactive matter. The exclusion in this subclause does not extend to radioactive isotopes, other than nuclear fuel, when such isotopes are being prepared, carried, stored, or used for commercial, agricultural, medical, scientific, or other similar peaceful purposes (e) any chemical, biological, bio-chemical, or electromagnetic weapon. 2. death, disability, loss, damage, destruction, any legal liabilities, cost or expense including consequential loss of whatsoever nature, directly or indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any of the following regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any other sequence to the loss: (a) war, invasion, acts of foreign enemies, hostilities or warlike operations (whether war be declared or not), civil war, rebellion, revolution, insurrection, civil commotion assuming the proportions of or amounting to an uprising, military or usurped power, or (b) any act of terrorism including but not limited to (i) the use or threat of force, violence and/or (ii) harm or damage to life or to property (or the threat of such harm or damage) including, but not limited to, nuclear radiation and/or contamination by chemical and/or biological agents, by any person(s) or group(s) of persons, committed for political, religious, ideological or similar purposes, express or otherwise, and/or to put the public or any section of the public in fear, or (c) any action taken in controlling, preventing, suppressing or in any way relating to (a) or (b) above. 3. Injury caused by the Insured Person engaging in: (a) air travel except as a passenger in a fully licensed passenger carrying aircraft (b) any trade, technical or sporting activity or as crew in connection with an aircraft 4. Injury caused by the Insured Person engaging in or practising for: (a) parachuting (b) hang gliding (c) any kind of race (other than on foot or swimming) or trial of speed or reliability (d) potholing, mountaineering or rock climbing necessitating the use of guides or ropes (e) underwater activities necessitating the use of compressed air or gas. 5. Injury caused by: (a) suicide, self-injury or wilful exposure to peril (other than in an attempt to save human life) (b) pregnancy or childbirth (c) insanity (d) any pre-existing physical or mental defect or infirmity (e) the Insured Person being under the influence of drugs (other than those prescribed by a registered Medical Practitioner but not when prescribed for the treatment of drug addiction) (f) the Insured Person being under the influence of alcohol, unless it can be established to our reasonable satisfaction by any claimant that alcohol was not a factor contributing to the happening of the Injury 6. Injury to any Insured Person who is employed: (a) as a full time military personnel, law enforcement officer, civil defence officer, security officer; navy, fire fighters or (b) as professional sportspersons, entertainers, motor vehicle or motor bike racers or (c) in any off-shore occupations such as diver, rig workers, fisherman; ship crew or (d) as shipyard crew; work on board sea vessels or (e) as air crew; work on board aircraft or (f) as construction workers, work at heights above 30 feet or work underground, in tunnels, demolition and quarry workers or (g) as workers engaged in maintenance, cleaning, roofing or repair activities involving scaffolding or gondolas; or (h) in any occupation dealing with explosives, poisonous or hazardous gases or substances If We say that by reason of any of these General Exceptions any claim is not covered by this insurance, then the burden of proving that the claim is covered shall be upon You. ABC Insurance Helpline (65) 6789 8181 A 24-hour emergency help line has been arranged for You with an authorised Help Centre should You need assistance in the event of an emergency whilst overseas. When You are residing in Singapore, the following services are also available to You - Arrangement of Home Visit by Physician - Arrangement of Private Medical Transportation - Arrangement of Appointment with the Medical Providers in Singapore It is understood and agreed that the emergency helpline only provides referral information and arrangement assistance. The service provider(s) is/are not Our agents and You shall pay the service provider directly for the costs of any service rendered by the service provider. It is further understood and agreed that We do not warrant and are in no way responsible or liable for the action of or advice or information given or statements made by any service provider or any person in the provision of service or assistance pursuant to the emergency helpline. We are also in no way liable for any injury, damage or loss to persons, property or goods whatsoever caused arising from any act, omission, default or neglect of the service provider or any person in the provision of service or assistance pursuant to the emergency helpline. PAYMENT BEFORE COVER WARRANTY 1. Notwithstanding anything herein contained but subject to clauses 2 and 3 hereof, it is hereby agreed and declared that the total premium due must be paid and actually received in full by the Company (or the intermediary through whom this Policy was effected) on or before the inception date (“the inception date”) of the coverage under the Policy, Renewal Certificate or Cover Note. 2. In the event that the total premium due is not paid and actually received in full by the Company (or the intermediary through whom this Policy was effected) on or before the inception date referred to above, then the Policy, Renewal Certificate, Cover Note and Endorsement shall not attach and no benefits whatsoever shall be payable by the Company. Any payment received thereafter shall be of no effect whatsoever as cover never attached on the Policy, Renewal Certificate, or Cover Note. 3. In respect of insurance coverage with "Free Look" provision, the Insured may return the original policy document to the Company or intermediary within the "Free Look" period if the Insured decides to cancel the cover during the "Free Look" period. In such an event, the Insured will receive a full refund of the premium paid to the Company provided that no claim has been made under the insurance. Policy Owners' Protection Scheme This policy is protected under the Policy Owners' Protection Scheme which is administered by the Singapore Deposit Insurance Corporation (SDIC). Coverage for your policy is automatic and no further action is required from you. For more information on the types of benefits that are covered under the scheme as well as the limits of coverage, where applicable, please contact your insurer or visit the GIA / LlA or SDIC websites (www.gia.org.sg or www.lia.org.sg or www.sdic.org.sg). IMPORTANT- You are requested to read this Policy. If any error or misdescription be found, the Policy should be returned to the issuing office for correction.
